title: Chief Operating Officer
description: **About Taxually** Taxually is a fast\-growing VAT and indirect tax compliance technology company serving 10,000\+ clients across 105\+ jurisdictions. We combine deep tax expertise with modern technology to simplify one of the most complex areas of global commerce. We're at an inflection point — scaling rapidly, embedding AI into our workflows, and building the infrastructure to support the next phase of growth. **The Role** We're looking for a Head of Operations to own the operational backbone of the company. This person will be responsible for ensuring Taxually runs efficiently, scales sustainably, and delivers consistently for clients — bringing structure and rigour to a business that's growing fast and evolving its operating model. This is a hands\-on leadership role. You'll work across Tax Operations, Customer Success, Business Support, and internal process design to drive throughput, quality, and cost efficiency. You'll partner closely with Product, Engineering, and the commercial team to ensure operational capacity keeps pace with growth and that we're deploying automation and AI where it matters most. **What You'll Do** * Operational leadership. Own the day\-to\-day operational engine — tax filing operations, client onboarding workflows, customer support delivery, and internal business support functions. Set and hold the standard for SLAs, turnaround times, and quality across every operational touchpoint. * Process design and continuous improvement. Audit and redesign core processes end\-to\-end. Identify bottlenecks, eliminate waste, and build repeatable playbooks that reduce manual effort and improve consistency as we scale across jurisdictions. * AI and automation strategy. Champion the integration of AI and automation into operational workflows. Work with Product and Engineering to prioritise, pilot, and roll out tools that reduce human\-in\-the\-loop dependency — particularly in client communications, document processing, and compliance workflows. * Performance management. Build and maintain an operational scorecard with clear KPIs across all functions. Establish a cadence of reporting and review that gives leadership real\-time visibility into operational health and flags issues early. * Team leadership. Lead, develop, and structure the operations team. Make smart decisions about headcount, role design, and capability building — balancing efficiency with the need to maintain deep domain expertise. * Cross\-functional coordination. Act as the connective tissue between commercial, product, and delivery. Ensure feedback loops exist between client\-facing teams and the teams building the product, so operational pain points translate into roadmap priorities. * Vendor and partner operations. Manage relationships with key external partners and service providers. Oversee outsourced or co\-delivered elements of the operating model to ensure quality and cost targets are met. * Scalability. Build operational models and capacity plans that support geographic expansion and new product lines without linear headcount growth. **What We're Looking For** * 8\+ years of experience in operations leadership, ideally in a B2B SaaS, fintech, or professional services environment with multi\-jurisdictional complexity. * Proven track record of scaling operations in a high\-growth company — you've built the machine, not just maintained it. * Strong process orientation. You think in systems, not tasks. You default to root\-cause analysis over firefighting. * Genuine fluency with data and metrics. You build dashboards, not just read them. * Direct experience deploying automation or AI in operational workflows. You understand what's possible and what's hype. * Excellent people leadership. You can attract, develop, and retain strong operators — and you're not afraid to make structural changes when the business requires it. * Commercial awareness. You understand that operations exists to serve the client and the P\&L, not the other way around. * Comfort with ambiguity. We're building and iterating — you'll need to make good decisions with imperfect information. **Nice to Have** * Experience in tax, accounting, or regulatory compliance operations. * Familiarity with the European VAT landscape or multi\-country compliance workflows. * Background working alongside or within product/engineering teams in a technology company. * Experience managing a transition from manual/human\-heavy operations to tech\-enabled delivery. **Why Taxually** * A category\-defining company in a massive, underserved market. * Real authority to shape how the company operates — this isn't a process\-maintenance role. * A leadership team that takes AI and automation seriously as a strategic lever, not a buzzword. * The chance to build operational infrastructure that supports global scale.

title: Civil Design Engineer
description: Civil Design Engineer **Build the Future of Power with Us! Your skills will contribute to impactful projects that shape the future.** **If you can turn complex ideas into practical designs, we want you on our team. At Powercomm, your work wont just sit in a file; it will shape the infrastructure of tomorrow.** **Job Summary:** We are seeking a talented Civil Design Engineer to join our growing team and play a key role in delivering integrated Civil and Electrical design services to clients across the Electrical and Renewable Engineering sector. This excellent opportunity offers the chance to contribute to diverse projects including substations, solar parks, wind farms, and battery storage facilities. As a Civil Design Engineer, you will support the development and expansion of our small civil design team while delivering quality design solutions. This role combines technical design expertise with team development responsibilities, providing an ideal platform for a motivated engineer to advance their career while helping to build capability within a growing design practice. **Job Responsibilities:** * Prepare and review Civil/Structural drawings in AutoCAD. Someone with 3D Modelling(Revit) experience is preferred. * Carry out and check Design calculations of civil works, including drainage design and site development works. * Produce technical report, specifications, and material schedules. * Coordinate with MEP engineers, contractors, clients, and other project stakeholders. * Support construction with technical queries, site visits, as\-built drawings, and handover documentation. * Train and mentor Graduate Design Engineers, overseeing their work, and checking their calculations. **Job Requirements:** * BSc/MSc in Civil/Structural Engineering or equivalent with post\-graduate design experience with an engineering consultancy. * Building structure design experience using all the main materials on a variety of structural projects but particular emphasis on Electrical Infrastructure, Transformer bunds, Foundations for Electrical Switchgear, new and existing Substation Buildings, Drainage, Roadways and Cable Basements. * Knowledge of Eurocodes and Irish/UK standards. * Experience with AutoCAD software and Revit. Experience with drainage software is often advantageous. * Knowledge of finite element and frame analysis software for steel and RC structure design work experience with Robot Structural Analysis software is preferred but not mandatory. * Level 8 degree in relevant field (Level 9 in advantageous). * Chartered status preferred. * 3\+Years of relevant experience

title: Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist
description: **Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist** **Salary: €63,000 \- €108,000 per annum** **Shift and schedule: Full\-time (37\.5 hours per week). Part\-time hours are also considered (minimum 3 days per week).** **Location: Gorey, County Wexford.** **Why Work with Evolve Ireland?** Be part of a supportive, purpose\-driven team dedicated to transforming neurodevelopmental care in Ireland and beyond. At Evolve, we offer: * A values\-led culture where clinical excellence, compassion, and staff wellbeing come first. * Continued professional development, clinical supervision, and reflective practice to support your ongoing clinical growth. * Competitive salary based on experience, with annual salary reviews and pension contributions. * Generous leave entitlement, including 28 days annual leave plus bank holidays \- and a day off for your birthday. * Private healthcare to support your physical and mental health * A company laptop and on\-call IT support to enable seamless remote and hybrid working. * Opportunities to contribute beyond clinical work via the Evolve Skills \& Research Academy: training, research \& content development. * Company events and a collaborative, multidisciplinary team passionate about improving outcomes for neurodivergent individuals and families in Ireland. **About the Role** At Evolve Ireland, we equip our clinicians with cutting\-edge tools and support so they can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care to children, young people, and their families. You’ll join a passionate and supportive multidisciplinary team dedicated to making a meaningful difference in the lives of children and young people. Our service specialises in high\-quality, evidence\-based assessments for autism, ADHD, intellectual disability, communication and coordination difficulties under the Assessment of Need pathway, alongside private work. As a Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist, you’ll play a central role in delivering comprehensive neurodevelopmental assessments, contributing your expertise in diagnostic formulation, differential diagnosis, and evidence\-based practice. You will help ensure timely, accurate reports that lead to appropriate supports and improved outcomes for children, adults and their families. **As a Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist, you will be responsible for:** * Conducting **face\-to\-face structured observations** as part of the neurodevelopmental Assessment of Need process. * Conducting **remote developmental history interviews** with parents and carers. * Participating collaboratively in **peer MDT diagnostic discussions**, supported by senior clinicians. * Delivering clear, compassionate **feedback** to families. * Producing accurate **clinical reports,** including **diagnostic formulations**, with the help of our smart tools. * Working efficiently to meet service **Key Performance Indicators** (KPIs). * Delivering clinical supervision (where applicable). * Travelling to our clinic base(s) to conduct in\-person assessments (hybrid). **Our Ways of Working** * **Digital workflows and smart report\-writing** reduce admin time and enhance clinical impact. * **A custom\-built electronic health record system,** developed in\-house using market leading research * **Continuously enhancing internal systems**, based on employee feedback, to improve efficiency * **Ongoing training and onboarding** to ensure confidence in all tech and systems * **In\-house IT and operational support**, so you're never left troubleshooting alone **Qualifications and Experience** * A recognised university degree or diploma (NQAI Level 8 equivalent) obtained with first or second\-class honours, in which psychology was taken as a major subject and honours obtained in that subject. * A postgraduate professional psychological qualification recognised by the Psychological Society of Ireland in Clinical or Counselling Psychology, OR a postgraduate professional psychology qualification validated by the Department of Health and Children in Clinical or Counselling Psychology. * For Educational Psychologists: a postgraduate qualification in Educational Psychology recognised by the PSI. * Minimum of 3 years’ experience conducting diagnostic assessments for autism and/or ADHD, or other neurodevelopmental conditions with children and young people OR/AND adults. * Strong understanding of differential diagnosis in neurodevelopmental presentations * In\-depth knowledge of DSM\-5 diagnostic criteria (or equivalent) for autism, ADHD, intellectual disability. * Experience conducting cognitive assessments for autism and/or ADHD, with children and young people OR/AND adults * Trained and experienced in the use of neurodevelopmental diagnostic tools such as ADOS\-2, ADI\-R, etc * Effective time management and caseload organisation skills, with the ability to manage competing demands and meet clinical deadlines * Highly developed written language skills used to produce high\-quality clinical reports * Well\-developed communication skills with children, young people, and their families, ensuring compassionate, clear, and professional interactions throughout the assessment process * Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team and participate in collaborative diagnostic discussions. * Must be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland. **About Evolve Ireland** At Evolve Ireland, we do things differently. Founded in 2014 by Dr Laura Powling, Evolve Psychology began with a bold aim to redefine what clinical and neurodevelopmental psychology means in practice. Laura’s vision was simple yet powerful: to bring care, connection, empathy, and clinical excellence together, with no compromise. What started as a small practice delivering expert witness and neurodevelopmental assessments has grown into an international independent psychology and neurodevelopmental service providing services across the UK, Ireland and beyond. In Ireland, we are nationally and regionally approved by the HSE. We also accept privately funded referrals. We specialise in a number of different pathways which include: * Neurodevelopmental assessments through the HSE Assessment of Need pathway, * Undertaking assessments under the National Autism Framework * Undertaking assessments referred from Children’s Disability Network Teams (CDNTs) * Providing adult neurodevelopmental assessments * Private neurodevelopmental assessments (online when clinically appropriate and in person) * SpLD (Dyslexia and Dyscalculia) assessments * Specialist individual assessments by Occupational Therapists and Speech and Language Therapists We are clinician founded and clinician led. Our clinicians are proudly registered with CORU or affiliated with the Psychological Society of Ireland and the Department of Health. Globally, we are an award\-winning, multidisciplinary team of over 100 experts, committed to delivering high\-quality expert services. **What sets Evolve Ireland apart?** * Expert leadership: Founded and led by Dr Laura Powling, a Consultant Clinical Psychologist with 20\+ years’ experience across mental health, psychological, forensic and neurodevelopmental services, bringing deep clinical insight and hands\-on leadership to everything we do. * National HSE approved provider: Recognised for the high\-quality services we provide, offering services regionally and nationally across Ireland * Dedicated Clinical Leadership \- Across all of our clinical pathways * Gold Standard Assessments compliant with NICE, PSI and other national and international assessment standards * Access to digital tools to support assessment delivery, including an electronic health record system * Comprehensive CPD and discipline\-specific clinical supervision offerings * We prioritise clinical quality and real impact, not speed or scale at the expense of care. Our assessments are evidence\-based, multidisciplinary, and designed to support the whole person, not just produce a diagnosis. * We offer more than assessment work. Our clinicians can contribute to the Evolve International Skills \& Research Academy, developing training and post\-assessment support for families and educational providers, shaping neuroinclusive content, or collaborating on research, all part of our broader mission to raise awareness and improve support for neurodivergent individuals, educational institutions and workplaces. * We have a strong, values\-led culture. Compassion, clarity, and integrity guide everything we do, from our team interactions to the way we support families. * Multi\-channel employee engagement\- A dedicated, holistic approach to employee engagement and wellbeing. Including online community information forum, team meetings, company events, open feedback forums, management Q\& A sessions, employee recognition and awards. * We’re growing sustainably, with purpose. With new clinics across Ireland targeting areas of most need and hard to reach communities. This includes hybrid and online assessments, where clinically appropriate. We are also a carbon\-neutral company. Pay: €63,000\.00\-€108,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking * Private medical insurance Ability to commute/relocate: * Gorey, County Wexford: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you ADOS and/or ADI\-R trained? * Do you have a valid DOH validation? * Are registered with the PSI? Experience: * Autism and/or ADHD assessment: 3 years (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Cloud Architect
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale cloud transformation programme and are seeking an experienced Cloud Architect to lead cloud design, governance, and compliance across complex regulated environments. This is a strategic role focused on delivering secure, scalable, and compliant cloud solutions while supporting enterprise\-wide migration and operational resilience initiatives. The successful candidate will work closely with engineering teams, security stakeholders, and programme leadership to define architecture standards and ensure alignment with regulatory and governance requirements. **Key Responsibilities** * Design secure enterprise cloud architectures, landing zones, and networking models * Lead cloud governance aligned to NIST\-aligned security frameworks, DORA, and GDPR requirements * Map application and integration dependencies to support migration planning and cutover readiness * Facilitate architecture workshops and stakeholder design reviews across engineering teams * Identify architectural risks and define remediation strategies for deployment and operational challenges * Support Infrastructure as Code frameworks and automated cloud deployment using Terraform and Ansible * Provide architectural guidance across cloud migration and transformation initiatives **Required Experience** * 7\+ years’ experience in enterprise cloud architecture, migration programmes, and governance frameworks * AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional * Strong understanding of NIST\-aligned cyber security frameworks, DORA, and GDPR * Expertise with Terraform, Ansible, IAM, VPC/VNet architecture, cloud connectivity, and encryption standards * Strong stakeholder engagement, governance, and solution design experience **Desirable Experience** * Insurance or financial services background * Experience working within highly regulated enterprise environments * Additional cloud certifications advantageous **How to Apply** If you are an experienced Cloud Architect, please apply with your latest CV.

title: Commercial Graduate Rotation Program - Benelux Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-Term Contract** **Duration:** 12\-month **Start Date: Aug 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give our graduates holistic learning and practical experience across both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* ****Who are we looking for?**** * You are fluent in English \& Dutch * You are a 2024 or 2025 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view ****What’s in it for you?**** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal equality groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1/1/1 model ****Map Your Future**** With our Graduate Program there are two optional career paths * The Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * The Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing ****Salesforce in Dublin**** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Commercial Support Executive
description: **The Role:** This position involves providing comprehensive administrative and operational support to the wholesale commercial team, contributing to the achievement of sales targets, and maintaining strong internal and external relationships. * Permanent Role * Location: Citywest **Key Responsibilities / Outcomes** * Support the activities of the Commercial team when dealing with manufacturers on a day to day basis. * Administration \& product set up duties to include data maintenance \& clean up activity as required. * Manage customer \& manufacturer queries and ensure they are logged and dealt with in a professional, timely manner. * Manage customer pricing queries in conjunction with the Uniphar Finance team. * To schedule and produce reports to required timelines and perform data analysis. * Liaise with all departments across the organisation to optimise the commercial offering and the pharmacy experience of service from the business. * Be day to day contact for a number of key customer group * Be main conjugate between Operations and Retail groups in managing cross orders, promotional stock and ad hoc queries. * Actively participate and contribute to business development and project initiatives as required. * Report weekly, monthly and quarterly both formally and informally, to an agreed timetable and format. * Operate as a team player and positively contribute to achieving growth targets of the business. * Support activities, including order processing, liaising with Operations on deliveries and dispatch **Requirements** * 1\-2 years experience in administration or operational support * Strong Excel skills * SAP experience is an advantage * Strong organisation skills * Excellent Communication skills **Key Outcomes and Responsibilities** **Strategic Partnership:** * Collaborate with business leaders to understand operational priorities and develop HR strategies to meet organisational goals in line with focus projects. * Provide guidance on workforce planning, talent management, employee engagement, and organisational design. * Serve as a ‘’consultant’’ on employee relations, performance management, and change management processes. **Project Management:** * Develop and deliver on detailed project plans, timelines, and budgets to ensure successful project delivery on time and in budget where applicable. * Coordinate cross\-functional teams and stakeholders, providing updates and resolving project challenges. * Review and analyse project outcomes and recommend improvements for future initiatives. * Utilise data and insights to assess project outcomes and refine approaches. **Talent and Workforce Development:** * Partner with leaders to design and implement strategies for talent acquisition, development, and retention on key change management projects. * Conduct training sessions, information sessions and workshops on HR\-related topics and initiatives project specific. **Data\-Driven Insights:** * Use HR analytics to identify trends, risks, and opportunities, providing actionable recommendations to leadership. * Monitor key HR metrics to track the effectiveness of initiatives and projects. **Compliance and Best Practices:** * Ensure HR policies and procedures comply with employment legislation. * Maintain knowledge of best practices in HR and contribute to continuous improvement efforts. * Ensure clear, transparent, and consistent messaging across the division **Skills \& Experiences** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field (Master’s preferred). * Proven experience as an HR Business Partner or in a similar role, with a focus on managing HR projects and change management. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across all levels of the organization * Excellent project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders effectively. * Strategic thinker with the ability to translate business objectives into actionable HR initiatives. * A progressive, non\-bureaucratic approach to HR * An ability to work in an agile way. Comfortable with ambiguity. * Enjoy working in an entrepreneurial culture that moves quickly and requires rapid response and the ability to make quick, sound decisions. * In\-depth knowledge of HR principles, practices, and legal requirements * Strong understanding of change management principles and experience leading organisational change initiatives * Ability to collaborate and build strong cross\-functional relationships. * An approach of listening carefully and asking questions to understand the why and the how of our current state while partnering to build our future state. * Patience and persistence in driving projects over the finish line against a landscape of regularly changing priorities. * Focused always on business impact and colleague experience. **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Company Secretary (In-House)
description: **Company Secretary (In\-House)** 📍 Dublin \| Hybrid An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Company Secretarial professional to join the governance function of a well\-established financial services organisation. This role offers exposure to Board and Committee governance, senior stakeholder engagement and a range of governance initiatives within a regulated environment. It would suit someone looking to build on their existing company secretarial experience while developing their career within a collaborative and highly professional team. **Key Responsibilities** * Supporting Board, Committee and governance meetings, including agenda preparation and board pack coordination. * Producing high\-quality minutes and maintaining accurate records of decisions and actions. * Managing matters arising and tracking governance actions through to completion. * Maintaining corporate records and supporting statutory and regulatory compliance requirements. * Assisting with governance projects, policy reviews and process improvements. * Building strong relationships with directors, senior management and key internal stakeholders. * Supporting the ongoing delivery of effective corporate governance across the organisation. **About You** * 3–5 years' experience in a Company Secretarial, Governance or related role. * Experience supporting Boards and Committees within a corporate or regulated environment. * Strong minute\-taking and organisational skills. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Comfortable interacting with senior stakeholders and directors. * CGI qualification (completed or in progress) would be advantageous but is not essential. * Experience within financial services, funds, banking, insurance or another regulated environment would be beneficial. **What's on Offer** * Opportunity to join a highly regarded governance team. * Exposure to senior stakeholders and meaningful governance projects. * Strong learning and development support. * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Hybrid working arrangement. Contact Alex O’Brien at alex.obrien@recruiters.ie or call \+353 1 522 5353 to discuss further in confidence. **By applying to this job, your CV will be assessed by a consultant for the position described, along with considering you for other relevant­ jobs as they arise in accordance with your interest, experience and skillset. Your CV may be added to our secure applicant tracking system however your details will never be shared to any other third party without your consent. You can browse a full spectrum of job opportunities and exercise your data subject rights anytime.**

title: Compliance Analyst
description: **Role Summary** **JOB DESCRIPTION** The Compliance Analyst will report to a Senior Compliance Manager on the Compliance team. They will be responsible for carrying out periodic tests in line with the Compliance monitoring plan. Testing will include key investor protection areas under MIFID II such as Suitability, Appropriateness, Product Governance and Client Categorisation among other areas. The Analyst will have the opportunity to interact with a wide range of business stakeholders in the role and provide compliance feedback and recommendations. The Analyst will present their work to the team at regular compliance monitoring meetings and contribute to enhancing the Cantor Fitzgerald investor protection framework on an ongoing basis. The Analyst will be responsible for reviewing marketing materials and will assist with other Compliance related tasks as required. Under The Central Bank Of Ireland’s (CBI) Individual Accountability Framework (IAF) Established Under The Central Bank (Individual Accountability Framework) Act 2023 (IAF Act), As An Expected Control Function Holder, You Have a Duty Of Responsibility Under The CBI Common Conduct Standards. These Standards Include * acting with honesty and integrity, * acting with due skill, care and diligence, * co\-operating in good faith and without delay with the Central Bank of Ireland * acting in the best interest of customers and treating them fairly and professionally * operating in compliance with standards of market conduct and trading venue rules **Responsibilities** **Key Responsibilities** * Execute monitoring tasks in line with the Compliance Monitoring Plan * Prepare output reports detailing observations, findings and recommendations for review by Senior Manager * Issuance of final reports to relevant first line functions and ensure findings are remediated where needed and brought to resolution * Present monitoring findings and recommendations as required * Prepare relevant management information for periodic Committee and Board reporting * Assist the Senior Manager in review and updating of compliance policies and procedures * Assist in the delivery of compliance training programme for the first line of defence * Asist in preparing regulatory responses to the Central Bank of Ireland * Review marketing materials prepared by the business * Assist with other Compliance tasks as required. **Skills \& Competencies** * Has a “can do” attitude and takes ownership of an issue from start to finish. * Offers practical advice to the business. * Uses experience, knowledge, and judgement to influence and deliver upon objectives. * Builds a sense of teamwork on his or her team and within/between groups. * Strives to improve him/herself and the Firm and actively seeks feedback from peers and management. * Has a willingness to grow within the Firm and to take on new responsibilities as required. **Requirements** **QUALIFICATIONS** * Third Level Qualification preferable. * Microsoft Office skills essential with strong Microsoft Excel skills a requirement. * Financial Services experience * Experience of Compliance Monitoring / Assurance Testing preferred * LCI qualification (or agreement to work towards same). **Please note that under the Central Bank of Ireland’s Fitness and Probity Standards, this role is a Control Function 2 (CF2\) role \-** **Ensuring, controlling, or monitoring** ***compliance*** **by a regulated financial service provider with its relevant obligations**

title: Compliance Officer
description: **About Waystone** Waystone is a leading asset\-servicing solutions provider of institutional governance, administration, risk and compliance services to financial institutions. With over 25 years’ experience and a comprehensive range of specialist services to its name, Waystone helps our clients structure, operate and grow through our expertise, innovation and digitisation, backed by the operational scale to support global expansion. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * Interpret and apply applicable laws, regulations, and regulatory guidance, with particular emphasis on AML/CFT requirements, escalating issues where appropriate * Monitor regulatory developments and support the implementation of new or revised regulatory requirements * Provide day‑to‑day compliance support and guidance to internal stakeholders across Operations, Risk, Legal, and other internal functions. * Act as a point of contact for day‑to‑day compliance queries, escalating more complex issues as required. * Support business initiatives, including new products, outsourcing arrangements, and change projects. * Draft, review, and maintain compliance and AML/CFT policies and procedures, ensuring documentation remains accurate and up to date. * Support the communication and implementation of policy and procedural updates across the business. * Support the execution of the Compliance Monitoring Programme, including thematic reviews, control testing, and ongoing compliance reviews, in line with approved plans. * Support the preparation and timely submission of regulatory filings, reports, and responses to regulatory information requests or inspections. * Assist in the preparation of compliance reports and materials for senior management, Group Compliance, Risk Committees, and the Board. * Review alerts, support investigations, and escalate suspicious activity in line with established procedures * Contribute to AML risk assessments and related reporting * Assist in identifying, logging, and investigating compliance breaches, and support remediation and follow‑up actions. * Maintain accurate, complete, and audit‑ready compliance records, registers, and supporting documentation. * Assist in the development and delivery of compliance and AML/CFT training, ensuring training records are properly maintained. * Promote awareness of regulatory and compliance obligations within the business. * Attend client and other external stakeholder meetings as the Compliance representative, contributing to due diligence reviews on compliance matters. * Represent the compliance function on external and internal working groups and committees, as required. * Support junior team members and contribute positively to team knowledge‑sharing and continuous improvement initiatives. The successful candidate will be a proactive and self‑motivated professional, capable of delivering high‑quality compliance support within a dynamic and fast‑paced regulatory environment. **They Will Demonstrate** * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage competing priorities effectively * A collaborative, solution‑oriented approach and the confidence to engage constructively with stakeholders at all levels * Excellent critical thinking, research, and interpretative skills, with the ability to apply regulatory requirements pragmatically * A high level of attention to detail and the ability to produce accurate, clear, and well‑structured work **Experience** * Minimum 5 years’ experience in a funds, fund administration, or regulated financial services compliance environment * Strong understanding of regulatory requirements applicable to the funds sector, with particular emphasis on AML/CFT * Experience in the design, implementation, and execution of: + Compliance policies and procedures + Compliance monitoring programmes **Education \& Qualifications** * Relevant compliance, AML, or financial crime qualification * University degree in Business, Law, Governance, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional qualifications or industry experience

title: Construction/Design Health & Safety Professional – PSDP
description: We are seeking highly motivated professionals to join our established PSDP team — based in **Dublin**. Hybrid working is supported, once suitably located to facilitate monthly site visits required across a wide range of live projects. You’ll work independently while reporting to the PSDP Associate Director and following robust QA procedures. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead the role of **Project Supervisor Design Process (PSDP)** on multiple projects. * Advise clients and design teams on SHWW (Construction) Regulations. * Identify and mitigate design\-related hazards. * Prepare hazard identification registers and Preliminary Safety \& Health Plans. * Coordinate with PSCS, contractors, and temporary works designers. * Attend site meetings and ensure compliance with safety legislation. * Compile and issue Safety Files upon project completion. * Contribute to continuous improvement of internal PSDP procedures. **What We’re Looking For** * **Minimum 3 years’ experience** in construction, engineering, or architectural roles. * Proven experience in a PSDP, PSCS, or similar H\&S advisory role. * Strong understanding of architectural drawings and construction site operations. * Excellent communication, reporting, and IT skills (MS Word \& Excel). * Ability to manage multiple projects and work independently. * Full clean driving licence and access to own vehicle. **Qualifications** * 3rd Level Diploma or Certificate in Safety, Health \& Welfare (e.g., HDip SHWW, CSHWW). * Chartered or working towards chartership with IOSH, RIAI, or IEI. * Construction\-related qualifications also considered. * Design related qualifications also considered once SHWW has been achieved. **What You Can Expect** * The chance to work within a high‑performing, collaborative team on a wide variety of project types. * A competitive salary package aligned with your experience and qualifications. * A supportive, dynamic workplace that values work‑life balance. * Opportunities for professional development through structured training and upskilling programmes. * Engagement with leading industry professionals and the opportunity to contribute to sustainable, forward‑thinking design. Reddy Architecture \& Urbanism is an equal opportunity employer committed to hiring a diverse workforce and sustaining an inclusive culture. Pay: €65,000\.00\-€75,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Contract .NET Developer – up to €500 per day

Contributed to the design, development, and continuous improvement of modern distributed applications, collaborating closely with cross\-functional teams to deliver high\-quality, maintainable software. Key Responsibilities * Designed and developed **maintainable, testable, and scalable backend services** using **.NET Core (C\#)** . * Delivered high\-quality code with **comprehensive unit and functional test coverage** . * Participated in **peer code reviews** and pull request processes to ensure coding standards, maintainability, and best practices. * Built and maintained **cloud\-native applications** in **Microsoft Azure environments** . * Worked with **message\-based distributed systems** to support asynchronous processing and scalable architectures. * Applied **software design principles and architectural best practices** when implementing solutions. * Followed **team development standards, coding guidelines, and .NET best practices** . * Collaborated with developers, QA, and DevOps engineers within **Agile delivery teams** . * Used **static code analysis tools** to ensure code quality and compliance with team standards. * Continuously improved development practices through **knowledge sharing and technical learning** . * Delivered features within **tight project deadlines** while maintaining high code quality. title: Corporate Solicitor
description: We have an exciting new opportunity for an **Associate Solicitor** to join a leading law firm in Dublin City Centre. Applications are invited from solicitors at associate level with a minimum of 3 years’ experience. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in mergers \& acquisitions and commercial transactions. This role will involve advising clients on complex legal issues, managing client relationships and contributing to the growth of the firm’s corporate practice. The firm has an excellent reputation as an employer with a strong sense of teamwork and fair play. Remuneration is very competitive and linked with experience. We pride ourselves in building long term relationships with our clients and have a great quality and diversity of work. The role will include work in the following areas: * Managing corporate transactions; * Drafting, reviewing and negotiating a variety of corporate documents, including share purchase agreements, shareholder agreements and various commercial agreements. * Managing, supervising and mentoring junior associates and trainee solicitors, including providing guidance on legal matters and their professional development. * Collaborating with clients to understand their business objectives and develop tailored legal strategies. * Staying updated on changes in corporate law and industry trends to provide informed advice. * Participating in business development activities. The successful candidate must have: * A minimum of 3 years’ experience in corporate law. * Strong knowledge of M\&A, commercial transactions and Irish company law. * Excellent drafting, negotiation and communication skills. * An ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines. * An ability to manage and supervise junior solicitors and trainees on various matters. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities. * Strong technical skills. * An ability to demonstrate clear communication skills with colleagues and clients. * An ability to work in a culturally diverse business, maintaining close working relationships with colleagues, clients, and business partners. * An excellent academic profile.

title: Customer Advisor - 30 Hours
description: **Your role in the JoJo story…** Do you dream of a role where you can make meaningful contributions that finish in days well spent? If you're ready to begin a fabulous adventure and you're passionate about retail, we have the perfect role for you! Each JoJo store is unique whether that’s trading over two floors, having a quirky layout or being situated in an interesting place. They have a life of their own. Our Unique Selling Point (USP) in this ever\-changing retail world is the relationship we build with our customers, many of them loyal to our brand and to their local store team. This is where you come in \- you’ll be the face of JoJo providing a fantastic level of customer care and service that we are renowned for and one we pride ourselves in upholding always. You’ll be guiding, informing, and assisting our customers in the correct purchase of our huge range of clothing and products. This could be an expectant parent, an excited grandparent or other friends or family. Our B Corp principles are so important, and every member of our teams will always uphold them. As we move into a digital retail world, offering an Omni\-Channel customer service is expected as this builds on customer loyalty. **A peek into your daily adventures:** * Assisting our customers in many different and sometimes diverse situations, from bra measuring or discussing our maternity wear, to demonstrating a baby carrier to an intrigued parent. * Preparing Omni\-Channel orders. * Liaising with customers in store or via the phone on Omni\-Channel orders. * Using our digital or virtual learning platforms to continuously develop your knowledge and retail skills. * Embracing new product knowledge or learning to increase sales opportunities. * Actively listening to our customers’ needs and working out what the best solution is for them. * Taking pride in keeping your store looking immaculate. ***A typical week would be 5 x 6 hour shifts however, flexibility is required in line with store requirements.*** **Skills that make you shine:** * Previous retail, customer service and sales experience * Competent IT skills and excellent systems ability, with an aptitude to pick up new systems quickly. * The ability to learn and retain a high level of product information and be able to communicate this effectively to our customers. * You’ll need a self\-developing mentality and a curiosity to learn new products independently. * We don’t like hard sales techniques, but you’ll need to demonstrate a level of sales ability, which includes active listening skills, empathy, and an understanding of our customers’ needs. * You’ll be working within a small team; therefore, the ability to work harmoniously and respectfully is important. * Working in a retail environment requires a level of flexibility, most of our stores are open 7 days a week. * We like to keep our stores looking tip\-top, so you’ll have a keen eye for detail and play a key role in maintaining our standard. Please note: We will only recruit individuals who are over the school leaver's age. To find out more about the school leaver age for Republic of Ireland\- Please click the following link: Rights of young workers (citizensinformation.ie) **Just to give you a taste of our culture, we’ll be looking for you to:** * Be a brand ambassador, safeguarding our values and ethos. * Demonstrate honesty and respect in every interaction. * Foster collaboration and open, active listening * Take ownership of all aspects of the role. * Proactively finding solutions to problems. * Leading a team. **Perks and Benefits:** * Extra day of annual leave for each year you’re with us, up to 10 additional days. * Is it your birthday? Take it off and celebrate! * Let’s celebrate JoJo doing well – non\-contractual bonus scheme. * Helping plan for the future \- enhanced pension scheme. * Flexible work options. * Enjoy fabulous social events. * Saying thank you – employee recognition programme. * Give back with volunteer days to support our communities. * Prioritise your well\-being with our Health and Wellbeing hub. * We love our products! Enjoy: + 40% off JoJo branded lines + 25% off full\-price NEXT products + 10% off NEXT Beauty products + Up to 10% off most branded products * Embrace an “It’s up to you” learning environment with ongoing training and development opportunities. **Want to know more about JoJo?** We make happy and helpful clothes and products that let children be children – in all their silly, messy, adventurous, noisy, imaginative joy. But we’re not just about selling products; we’re about creating a warm, community\-centric environment for our employees and our customers. From 39 locations across the UK and Ireland and a thriving e\-commerce platform, we offer a meticulously designed collection of children’s clothing, toys, and essentials designed with love and care: always with children at the forefront of our minds. As a B Corp, our commitment goes beyond commerce, we strive for a sustainable future and take pride in our responsibilities to the planet and our communities. At JoJo, every role, every task and every day is an opportunity to support, inspire and contribute to the well\-being of our teams and our customers. We embrace and openly welcome people from different cultures, perspectives, and experiences to create a diverse and enriched environment. We work hard to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people, regardless of background to make sure we grow our company sustainably and with different cultural influences. By encouraging a diverse and inclusive workforce, we aim to create an environment where everyone can thrive and reach their full potential.

title: Customer Support
description: At **Manna** , we're changing how the world receives things — by taking delivery to the skies. Our mission is simple but ambitious: **to make high\-speed suburban delivery affordable, green, and safe** . We design and build our own aviation\-grade drones that deliver food, coffee, groceries, and more — directly from local stores and vendors to people's doors in just a few minutes. We're a team that **Drive with Impact** , are **Safety Focused** , and knows that the best ideas come from **Team Players** who show up as their **Authentic Selves** . We move fast, we are passionate about what we do, and we're always pushing a growth mindset to **be better** — in everything we do. If you want to build world\-changing technology with real\-world impact (and have a bit of fun while doing it), you'll love it here. **Welcome to Manna — where we deliver the future.** **Requirements** **Key Responsibilities and Duties:** * Responsible for handling all enquiries on intercom pertaining to Manna issues across all our locations * Liaison between customer and vendor when the issue lies outside of Manna's remit * Tracking common issues and disseminating to ops teams to ensure we are safeguarding against them in the future * End to End\-to\-end ownership of a reported issue, working with stakeholders across the business to ensure it is resolved in a timely fashion * Coaching loaders on how to prevent future issues based on your customer experience * Other reasonable ad hoc customer success projects as required * Presentation of orders/ training new staff \- on customer excellence **Key Skills and Competencies:** * You're a self\-starter who's comfortable working in ambiguity and adapting to changing priorities * You have strong people skills and a genuine desire to help others have positive experiences * You communicate clearly and confidently with a wide range of stakeholders * You stay calm under pressure and handle challenging situations with professionalism and empathy * You're organised, proactive, and a collaborative team player who's happy to roll up your sleeves to get the job done €15\.50 per hour

title: Cyber Data Analyst / Detection Analyst
description: **Role Purpose:** Develop actionable insights and detection rules from cyber datasets. **Key Responsibilities** · Analyse logs (network, endpoints, identity, transactions) · Build anomaly detection queries and rules · Create dashboards (threat monitoring, incident trends) · Support SOC teams with investigative insights · Perform ad\-hoc deep dives on incidents **Required Skills** · SQL, Python (basic analytics) · Power BI Skills · Experience with SIEM tools (Splunk, Sentinel, QRadar) · Strong data interpretation and pattern recognition · Familiarity with cyber threats and attack vectors **Key Deliverables** · Detection rules / queries · Insight reports \& dashboards · Incident analysis outputs

title: Data Quality Analyst
description: **About us:** Amach is an industry\-leading technology driven company with headquarters located in Dublin and remote teams in UK and Europe. Our blended teams of local and nearshore talent are optimised to deliver high quality and collaborative solutions. Founded in 2013, Amach was created to solve a specific problem in aviation: too much complexity, too little usable intelligence. We help airlines modernise their operating model using cloud, data and Al\-delivered by teams with deep aviation domain expertise. Our goal is to maximize airlines' operational efficiency by optimizing resource use, reduce costs and increase customer experience and satisfaction. We are seeking a **Data Quality Analyst** to be embedded with our customer, where you will drive improvements in data quality, trust, and accountability across their organisation. Working closely with the customer's teams, you will identify critical data issues, define and monitor quality rules, investigate root causes, and partner with data owners and stewards to deliver sustainable improvements. This is a hands\-on role that combines data quality management, governance, and stakeholder engagement to support the customer's strategic data objectives. ***Please note** : this is a fully remote role.* **Key responsibilities \& duties include:** * Gather and document business requirements for data quality monitoring and governance initiatives working with the customer's stakeholders * Support the triage, prioritisation, investigation, and remediation of data quality issues and incidents across the customer's data landscape * Assist with the maintenance, adoption, and continuous improvement of the customer's enterprise data catalogue * Build strong working relationships with the customer's business stakeholders, data owners, and data stewards to support data quality improvement initiatives * Manage stakeholder communications throughout the lifecycle of data quality projects and initiatives with the customer's teams * Analyse the customer's existing databases, systems, and title: Deal Desk Analyst (Hybrid)
description: ****About Us:**** A global SaaS leader for seamless digital document workflows, Nitro offers a suite of solutions for PDF, eSigning, identity verification and analytics supported by a best\-in\-class customer success and change management team. With more than 3 million licensed users and 13,000\+ business customers across 157 countries, we serve 67% of the Fortune 500\. ****How We Work:**** We aim to create an environment where talented individuals are empowered to excel. How we collaborate, innovate, and engage with one another is important to us. Our work is driven by 5 key principles: * One team, One mission Our collective dedication to Nitro's mission defines us. Together, we are building an environment where everyone feels like a valued part of something bigger than themselves. * Own it We take full ownership of our actions and decisions. We empower one another to lead with confidence, creativity, and a solutions\-focused mindset. * Accountable to our customers We are dedicated to our customers and take our commitments seriously. We do what we say we are going to do. * Excellence in execution Driven by passion and precision, we exemplify excellence in our delivery with innovative, top\-quality results. * Be bold, fail fast, learn faster We learn as we grow, dare to try, and bravely question. We are not chasing perfection but forever iterating towards it. These guiding values shape our approach to work, fostering a culture where everyone is inspired to contribute their best. ****The Role:**** The Deal Desk Analyst partners closely with Sales, Finance, and other cross\-functional teams to ensure all deals are structured, approved, and executed efficiently and in compliance with company policies. This role plays a critical part in enabling scalable growth by improving deal quality, streamlining processes, and supporting the end\-to\-end quote\-to\-cash lifecycle. ****What You'll Be Doing:**** * Act as a trusted advisor to Sales, guiding contract structuring and negotiation strategy, including pricing and compliant deal structuring alternatives * Manage the processing and closing of daily sales transactions using Salesforce CPQ and billing systems * Deliver customer licensing communications and support the setup of customer admin portals * Coordinate with stakeholders to seek input and approvals for complex deals * Assist sales teams with RPF/RFQ responses, including helping to maintain common questions policies * Help evolve deal desk processes, improving how we manage non\- standard deals and customer\-specific requirements What We're Looking For: * 2\+ years of operations experience with technology companies in functions such as Deal Desk , Revops or SalesOps with knowledge of Revenue principles and or contracts management * Comfortable working with systems such as Salesforce CPQ, Chargebee and billing systems * Curious, proactive self\-starter with strong problem\-solving skills * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities * Commercially aware with an understanding of deal structuring principles **Why Nitro?** Along With Our Regular Benefits And Programs (including Health, Dental, Vision, And Retirement As Standard), We Are Also Very Proud To Offer a Few Additional Initiatives To Future Nitronauts: **Flex Time Off** Work\-life balance is important at Nitro, and we understand that there are events that we cannot plan for. We are proud to offer Flex Time Off to be used for holidays, spending days with your family, or appointments. **Hybrid Work** Our team embraces the hybrid work model, appreciating its blend of flexibility and structure. We combine three days of in\-person collaboration at our global offices in Toronto, Dublin, Antwerp, Porto, and Melbourne with the convenience of two days of remote work each week. ****Benefits:**** Nitro provides all employees with a comprehensive benefits package that includes health insurance, dental and vision coverage, and wellness perks. We also offer pension/401k matching, along with many other country\-specific benefits. *Nitro strongly encourages applications from everyone regardless of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status. We provide an accessible candidate experience and invite you to request any accommodations or adjustments throughout the interview process and beyond.*

title: Design Engineer (Civil)
description: **Design Build Search** is seeking a **Design Engineer (Civil)** on behalf of our client based in Dublin 15\. This opportunity is suited to candidates who holds a minimum Level 8 Honours Degree in Civil or Structural Engineering, with approximately 3 to 6 years’ post\-graduate experience. **Responsibilities:** Working as part of a collaborative engineering team, and with guidance from senior staff, the successful candidate will: * Engage with clients, under the direction of the Project Manager, to deliver design solutions that meet project requirements. * Review and provide technical oversight of documentation and designs prepared by Graduate Engineers. * Prepare and check preliminary designs, technical reports, and detailed design drawings/specifications using a range of industry\-standard software packages. * Coordinate and manage the procurement process for Site Investigations and Building/Civil Engineering contracts. * Support Project Managers in the preparation of tender documentation and the evaluation of submitted tenders. * Assist in the administration of construction contracts, ensuring compliance with design intent, programme timelines, and budgetary constraints. * Report to and take direction from their Line Manager, Project Managers, and senior project team members as required. **Requirements:** * Strong written and verbal communication skills in English, along with effective interpersonal abilities. * Proficiency in AutoCAD 2D, with a willingness and aptitude to further develop skills in AutoCAD 3D and BIM platforms such as Autodesk Revit. * An interest in developing competence in additional software tools, including MicroDrainage, Microsoft Access, and GIS applications such as ArcGIS and MapInfo. * A working understanding of construction contract frameworks, including the Public Works Management Framework (PWMF) forms of contract. * Familiarity with relevant project and design software such as Microsoft Project, MicroDrainage, and other tools necessary to effectively perform daily responsibilities. This role presents an excellent opportunity for a Design Engineer seeking to further develop their career within a well\-established and reputable engineering consultancy.
