title: Graduate QA Test Engineer
description: **Role overview** Kick\-start your career in software quality at DOCOsoft, a Dublin\-based InsurTech company whose claims management platform processes billions in data for some of the world's largest insurers. As a Graduate QA Engineer, you will learn to break complex systems before customers ever see them, build automation from day one, and grow into a well\-rounded engineer with exposure across the full software development lifecycle. You will join a collaborative QA team that plays a critical role in every release. Working alongside developers, business analysts, and DevOps engineers, you will analyse requirements, design and execute tests, develop automation, and champion quality across the organisation. At DOCOsoft, no two days look the same and we actively encourage our people to contribute ideas, challenge assumptions, and explore different areas of the business. **About DOCOsoft** DOCOsoft is the leading provider of claims management software to the London insurance market. Our platform is trusted by over 20 of the world's top\-tier insurers, handling more than £10 billion in claims annually. Founded in Dublin in 2008, we now employ over 100 people across a range of disciplines and are in an exciting growth phase \- launching our new SaaS platform, DOCOsoft Vew, and expanding into international markets. Our culture is built on five core values: Collaborative, Committed, Trusted, Creative, and Grounded. We combine Irish hard work and community spirit with a philosophy of continuous improvement, and we invest in our people so they can do their best work. **Responsibilities** * Test and validate our platform to ensure it meets the highest quality standards before every customer release. * Design, write, and maintain automated test suites using Playwright. * Analyse requirements and user stories to create detailed, traceable test cases. * Investigate, reproduce, and clearly document defects so development teams can resolve issues efficiently. * Collaborate with developers, product owners, and DevOps engineers across multiple project teams. * Propose and pilot improvements to our testing processes, tools, and overall quality strategy. * Contribute to API and database\-level testing to ensure data integrity across our integrations. * Manage your time effectively across concurrent projects, communicating progress and risks proactively. **What You'll Learn in Your First Year:** We are investing in your growth from day one. In your first twelve months, you can expect to: * Gain hands\-on experience building Playwright automation suites on real client projects. * Develop a strong understanding of the insurance domain — how complex claims data is received, processed, and presented to end users. * Learn how CI/CD pipelines work and how quality gates protect production environments. * Pair with senior QA engineers and developers through a structured mentorship programme. * Carry out Non\-Functional testing activities (performance testing and security testing. * Participate in Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives. * Present your findings and testing insights to cross\-functional teams. * Explore other areas of the business — we encourage involvement in DevOps, product, and innovation initiatives. **Key Requirements:** We value curiosity, initiative, and a genuine passion for quality over a long list of credentials. If you recognise yourself in the following, we would love to hear from you: * A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related discipline (or equivalent practical experience such as a coding bootcamp or strong personal projects). * 0–2 years of relevant experience — internships, college projects, and personal work all count. * A natural curiosity and a knack for spotting what others miss. * Comfort reading and writing code — you do not need to be an expert, but you are not afraid of a codebase. * Strong communication skills — you can clearly explain what is broken and why it matters. * A collaborative mindset and a willingness to both give and receive constructive feedback. We realise applying for jobs can feel daunting. Even if you do not tick every box above, we encourage you to apply \- the best candidate does not always check every single requirement. **Our Tech Stack** **You will work with the following technologies and tools:** * C\# and/or other object\-oriented programming languages. * Client\-side technologies: JavaScript and TypeScript. * Automation frameworks: Playwright, Selenium. * SQL Server and relational database querying. * Git, Azure DevOps for version control and work tracking. * Microsoft Visual Studio Code or similar IDEs. * API testing tools: Postman, REST APIs. * Load and performance testing (JMeter, K6\). * CI/CD pipelines and build automation. * Cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS). * Unit testing frameworks. * AI\-assisted development tools (e.g. GitHub Copilot). **Who we are:** DOCOsoft is a leading software and services provider to Lloyd’s of London and the broader London insurance market. Since our foundation, we have grown to become one of the leading insurance software specialists in the London Insurance Market. We are a growing team of over 100 colleagues based in Dublin, London, Tokyo, Portugal, Spain, India and Poland. **Here’s what we have to offer:** DOCOsoft aspires to be a market leader in the technology sector, and we are always looking for new ways to approach projects or improve existing content. We look to hire people that will help us achieve this with hard work, enthusiasm and an expression of their own ideas. We offer our people the opportunity to impact our growing business\- everyone’s contribution matters! A team environment that is focused on the creation and delivery of great products for our clients. Exciting challenges to grow and enhance their skills and a competitive pay and benefits package including; * + 25 days Annual Leave, + Private pension, + Bonus scheme, + Private health, + Life assurance. **Equal Opportunity Employer** DOCOsoft is committed to building an inclusive and diverse team that represents a variety of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, and do not discriminate on the grounds of race, religion, gender, marital or family status, age, disability, sexual orientation, membership of the travelling community or any other basis as protected by applicable law. Should you require reasonable accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Group Financial Accountant
description: Join Keelings! You will be part of a passionate team of people and a growing business. Come on the journey with us where you will grow and develop and truly feel a sense of belonging. At Keelings, we don't just grow quality fresh produce, we also nurture people and their professional development. The Keelings family always employ the best people for the job, the key to our success is the quality of the people we employ. **Purpose Of The Role** This role is within the Group Finance function. The successful candidate will play a key role within the areas of Group reporting, assisting in the management of annual audit, internal governance reviews and ensuring compliance with Group Policy \& procedures as well as a wide range of other responsibilities. This is an opportunity for an enthusiastic and ambitious qualified accountant to learn and get exposure to many financial aspects of a large fast\-moving business. **Key Accountabilities \& Responsibilities** Duties will include some of the following tasks but may be subject to change: * Assisting with management of the annual external audit * Assisting with preparation of the monthly consolidation * Assisting in conducting balance sheet reviews of all group operating companies once per quarter * and summarising findings back to CFO as well as Audit, Risk \& Finance Committee * Conducting detailed control reviews as directed by CFO / Group Financial Controller * Responsible for updating of Policies and procedures across the Group as directed by CFO and Group Financial Controller * Responsibility for identification of eligible grants and assisting operating Finance teams in the successful completion of claims and were necessary, co\-ordinate the assistance of external specialists to assist in the process * Responsible for completion of all reporting to Agri Food Regulator as well as Enterprise Ireland * Approving weekly supplier and payroll payments * Approving FX trades on daily basis * Ad hoc projects and assisting with systems implementations and upgrades **Essential** **Key Skills \& Knowledge** * To have an open mindset and a willingness to grasp responsibility as offered and deliver on tasks assigned particularly when these are challenging and new * Practice background with experience of audit and financial statements a necessity * Masters level qualification on Accountancy or Business * Comfortable interacting with senior management and other functional areas * Good communication \& presentation skills **Desirable** * Previous experience of governance / internal audit would be a distinct advantage * Ability to work on own initiative and as well as part of team * Ability to manage time and work to strict deadlines * Attention to detail and a desire to complete work to a high standard is a prerequisite * Excellent IT skills required, experience of RPA and/or VBA an advantage but not required * Experience of SUN / Infor Accounting system an advantage * Ability to speak French, Portuguese or Spanish an advantage but not required. * FMCG experience preferred. **Keelings Values** * People Matter \- We support and treat each other in a clear, fair and respectful way. We all learn, develop and encourage everyone to contribute * Teamwork \- We inspire and help each other to deliver our ambitions together as One Keelings * Integrity \- We do the right thing and deliver on our commitments * Passion for Achievement \- We motivate ourselves and each other to achieve our ambitions with energy, knowledge and courage. We are always striving to be better. **Recruitment Process** * CV Submission \& email acknowledgment of receipt * Phone Screening * 1st Interview * 2nd Interview * Outcome \& Feedback **Inclusion \& Diversity Statement** In Keelings we value all perspectives and knowledge that our colleagues bring to our business. We are committed to forming a diverse and inclusive team where all can share their unique experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all cultures and backgrounds and are dedicated to forming an environment of inclusiveness and diversity where all can thoroughly feel a sense of belonging. **Reasonable Accommodation Statement** If you require reasonable accommodation in submitting your application, interviewing, completing any pre\-employment testing, or otherwise participating in the recruitment process, please contact recruitment\_keelings@keelings.com Keelings Careers \- https://www.keelings.com/careers\-keelings/ Keelings Strategy \& Values – https://www.keelings.com/our\-people

title: Human Resources Generalist- 4.5 days per week
description: **Location:** Weatherwell Industrial Estate, Clondalkin **Reports To:** Head of HR **Full\-Time Permanent** (4\.5 days/week). Onsite as our workforce is onsite and we support them **Hours:** 37 hours week Mon to Thurs: 8:00am \- 4:30pm. Early finish most Fridays @ 1:00pm, no weekends required. **Salary:** €50,000 to 55,000 for the 37 hours Due to continued expansion, we are hiring a Human Resources Generalist reporting to the Head of HR. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering HR services across our Clondalkin and Cherry Orchard sites in Dublin, enabling the design and production of low voltage electrical power distribution systems for both local and multinational clients. This position is ideal for someone with minimum 3 years of experience in a dedicated HR role who wants broad, hands\-on exposure across HR and thrives on tackling a variety of challenging situations. We looking for a true generalist who is a competent, confident HR professional who can operate credibly with all employees, taking ownership of complex matters, and applying sound judgement grounded in legislation, policy, and best practice. **Key Responsibilities** **Employee Relations \& HR Advisory** * Attend weekly or bi weekly meetings with various managers regarding business progress in the context of HR ensuring compliance with Irish employment legislation and best practice. * Act as a trusted advisor, coaching leaders on HR\-related issues with a professional and introspective approach towards performance , absence management, conduct and other related matters. * Independently handling investigations such as grievance and disciplinary processes, applying the principles of fair and natural justice, ensuring compliant documentation aligned to Irish employment law and best practice. * Provide guidance to management and employees on all HR matters, * Maintain consistency in policy application and precedent, ensuring fairness and legal compliance. **Learning \& Development Delivery** * Deliver new starter induction presentations and reinductions as required. * Work closely with the Head of HR to design, deliver and evaluate in\-person and online training programs using our new LMS, covering technical, compliance, and soft\-skills training. Maintain accurate, centralized training records to support group reporting, development tracking and compliance requirements. * Support leadership development and workforce upskilling by partnering with the Leadership team and HR team to identify current and future capability needs. * Work closely with managers to identify learning needs and development opportunities across the organisation. **HR Operations \& Compliance** * Apply policy, precedent, and legislation consistently, understanding the risk and impact of decisions on the business. * Act as the first point of escalation for operational HR issues, exercising sound judgement and knowing when to step back. * Co\-own with our HR Admin the day\-to\-day HR operations across the employee lifecycle, including onboarding, contract management, probation reviews, role changes, and leavers. * Prepare and analyse HR metrics (absence, turnover, ER trends, training compliance), providing insight rather than just data. * Continuously review, draft, implement, and interpret HR policies and procedures, ensuring alignment with Irish employment legislation, codes of practice, and case law, and applying them consistently in live, day\-to\-day situations. * Work closely with the HR Admin to maintain accurate and legally compliant employee records, ensuring GDPR standards are met at all times. Ensuring documentation and processes stand up to required scrutiny. * Work closely with the HR Admin to Manage HRIS systems end\-to\-end, including data integrity, reporting, audits, and process improvements. * Ensure employment contracts, letters, and documentation are fit for purpose, legally compliant, and commercially appropriate. **Culture, Engagement \& CSR** * Coordinate and support initiatives that enhance employee engagement, wellbeing, and inclusion. * Drive contributions to corporate social responsibility programs and projects that strengthen company culture. * Champion team\-building, employee recognition programs, CSR activities and effective internal communication to foster a positive workplace environment. **Projects \& Continuous Improvement** * Own and deliver HR projects aligned with the 24\-month HR Project Plan, focused on improving employee experience, HR processes, and systems. * Present findings, analyze trends, identify opportunities, and implement practical solutions that add business value. * Collaborate with HR colleagues to ensure cohesive and coordinated project delivery across the team. **Recruitment \& Talent Acquisition** * Focus remains on broader HR responsibilities; day\-to\-day recruitment is handled by our dedicated recruiter. This role occasionally supports recruitment for specialist roles, internal promotions, or internal moves. * Advising managers on specs, selection and assessment for these specific cases, ensuring fair and effective decisions are applied. **Essential Qualifications \& Experience:** * 3 years proven HR experience in a local business operating within a multinational environment. * Broad, hands\-on experience across HR functions including employee relations, learning \& development, recruitment (supporting specialist/internal roles), and compliance. * Strong knowledge of Irish employment law, codes of practice, and HR best practices. * Experience drafting HR policies and procedures, applying them consistently to various situations. * Confident handling complex employee relations, including grievances, disciplinaries, and performance issues. * Demonstrated ability to coach and influence management in professional HR practices. * Experience delivering learning modules and development programs. * Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. **Essential Experience \& Qualifications** * Professional HR qualification (e.g., CIPD) or 3\+ years’ equivalent experience in a broad HR role. * Experience handling employee relations, L\&D, HR operations, and compliance within a local business, it would be an advantage if this was part of an MNC environment. * Strong familiarity with HRIS systems and reporting platforms; experience with docking systems is an advantage. * Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced, hands\-on environment. **Additional Experience \& Competencies** * Exposure to manufacturing or on\-site operational environments is an advantage. * Experience in employee engagement or culture initiatives. * Business\-minded, with a strong sense of accountability. * Policy and precedent driven, applying guidance consistently and fairly. * Highly professional, diplomatic, and introspective in communication and coaching. * Collaborative team player, able to operate independently and take ownership. * Passionate about HR as a career, motivated by variety, challenge, and responsibility. * Comfortable working on\-site and supporting operational teams directly **What We Offer** * An engaging and collaborative HR team environment. * Exposure to the full HR spectrum in a varied, fast\-paced business. * Good work life balance with a 4\.5 day week. * Opportunities to lead projects, shape culture, and develop professionally. * Regular team interactions and 1:1 coaching with your HR leader. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €50,000\.00\-€55,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * DUBLIN 22, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Will you now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status? * Are you satisfied this is a fully onsite role? title: IT Infrastructure Engineer

title: IT Solutions Architect
description: ### **Job Title** IT Solutions Architect ### **Salary** €76, 202 pa (Other arrangements may apply of you are currently a serving civil or public servant) ### **Tenure** 5\-year fixed term contract ### **Work Location** Dublin office ### **Closing Date** Midnight, Thursday 2nd July 2026 ### **Role Profile** We have an exciting opportunity in SEAI for a Solutions Architect in the IT Department which is part of the Corporate Services Directorate. The IT Department provides support across all aspects of SEAI operations, with teams focused on several areas: IT Project and Business System Programmes (PMO), Enterprise Architecture, Data Governance, IT Operations including IT Helpdesk, IT Governance, IT Change Management, and Cyber and Information Security. The successful candidate will join the current Enterprise Architect and support them in defining, maintaining, and governing the SEAIs IT systems architecture to ensure that business strategy objectives, and costeffective technology are aligned. Reporting to the Chief Information Officer (CIO), the successful candidate will work across the IT Department and third\-party vendors to develop and integrate ICT systems, reviewing existing systems to identify areas for improvement and monitoring the outcome of new and upgrades business systems. The successful candidate will join an enthusiastic and innovative team who are committed to the high\-quality delivery of SEAI's ambitious ICT Strategy and customer centric digital services. ### **Essential Requirements** The successful candidate must be able to demonstrate: * A degree (minimum Level 8 on the NFQ or higher) in IT, Computer Science, or other relevant discipline. * A minimum of 5 years directly relevant experience working in IT Architecture design and implementation within large complex organisations (a master's degree may account for 1\-years' experience). * Demonstrated proficiency in system architecture principles and frameworks, software development practices, cloud platforms (Azure) and service orientated architecture (SOA), data architecture and software development practices. * Experience in establishing and operating architecture frameworks and governance**.** * Significant experience in stakeholder management involving a diverse range of internal and external stakeholders with complex interdependencies. * Proficiency in development methodologies such as DevOps. ### **Desirable Requirements** * Certificate in architecture frameworks such as TOGAF. * Experience in enterprise\-level systems design. * Experience in microservices and API first architectures. * Proficiency in the Irish Language. * Experience or knowledge of delivering positive customer experiences. ### **Eligibility to Work** The SEAI has a legal obligation to ensure that all employees are lawfully entitled to work in Ireland. To qualify, candidates must hold a valid visa on the date of application and where applicable to support a candidate's application, candidates must submit a valid work permit/visa confirming permission to work in Ireland when requested. Failure to submit the required evidence, and failure to hold a valid permit/visa during the period of the contract, will result in the application and/or contract of employment being rendered void. Note in respect of UK citizens: Information regarding the Common Travel Area is available **here**. **\#LI\-Hybrid**

**Task Prioritization:** Ability to prioritize tasks based on the severity and impact of technical issues. * **Multitasking:** Managing multiple incidents or service requests at the same time while products. * **Warranty and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Management:** Handling warranty claims and equipment replacements through vendor support. * **Certifications:** **Essential** * title: Junior Associate - Investment Servicing
description: Summary: The Junior Associate will assist the Middle Office team in the development of large\-scale projects for high profile platforms, from Management Companies to fund clients and work in a supportive role within the Trade Execution and Investment Servicing teams. * Adhere to and follow the middle office checklist daily, including but not limited to Corporate Actions, Data Checks, Subscriptions and Redemption activity, Unfilled Order and Pending Instructions, Failed Trades, New Order Instructions and Reconciliations. * Assist the Manager with the design and implementation of internal and operating controls (especially in relation to OMS and PMS systems and all interactions with the market. * As you grown, learn to evaluate the risk involved in each situation, prioritize risk levels and escalate as appropriate. * Assist the team in ensuring all securities are settled on time and failed trades are investigated. * Currency Hedging including the daily production of the hedge fund workbook for review by the supervisor and instruction of hedge adjustments required which must be approved by supervisor. * Daily reconciliation between Middle Office records and depositary/custodian holdings. * Liaise daily with the trade execution team to ensure the trade execution, settlement and reconciliation process is adhered to. * Follow all pre\-trade compliance procedures and checks, escalate breaches. * Assist in the on\-boarding of new clients, broker and depositary/custodians. * As instructed by your client and approved by the supervisor, structure and process all payments across multiple platforms and service providers. * Ensure all corporate actions (elective and mandatory) are processed and accounted for. * Actively participate in fund governance meetings and attend technical training. * Assists colleagues when capacity permits and notifies supervisor with availability. * Foster positive team environment and ensure open communication. * Actively participate in team projects as instructed by the supervisor. * Develop and maintain detailed knowledge of Waystone operations and the regulatory implications thereof. * Adhere to Waystone change management procedures in order to ensure efficient implementations and minimal client impact. * Assist the team in the development, implementation and monitoring of policies and procedural manuals that contribute to the efficiency of the department while also ensuring timely and accurate delivery of service and regulatory compliance. * Ensure all regulatory and compliance requirements are met for an assigned portfolio of clients. * Communicate with clients to ensure satisfaction with the service provided and to resolve any potential issues. * When instructed, independently liaise with fund service providers and fund legal counsels. To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. **Education** * A third level degree in Business, finance and/or related area. **Experience** * Ideally, candidates will have some prior hedge fund documentation and client service exposure or have a paralegal or corporate administrator background. * Have a strong interest in the Financial Services Sector. * Outstanding time management, organizational skills, oral/written communication skills, critical thinking and attention to detail is required. * Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment including reporting and providing support to various members of the team. * Strong MS Office and Analytic Skills; mid\-level information management software experience required.

description: **About Marex** Marex Group plc (NASDAQ: MRX) is a diversified global financial services platform providing essential liquidity, market access and infrastructure services to clients across energy, commodities and financial markets. The group provides comprehensive breadth and depth of coverage across four core services: clearing, agency and execution, market making, and hedging and investment solutions. It has a leading franchise in many major metals, energy and agricultural products, with access to 60 exchanges. The group provides access to the world's major commodity markets, covering a broad range of clients that include some of the largest commodity producers, consumers and traders, banks, hedge funds and asset managers. With more than 40 offices worldwide, the group has over 2,300 employees across Europe, Asia and the Americas. For more information visit https://www.marex.com/ **Role Summary** Are you an ambitious, proactive individual with a strong interest in sales and the energy industry? We are seeking a driven talent to join our team as Junior Energy Broker. In this role, you will be trained to build relationships and arrange transactions between Marex's clients in European power and gas. This is an excellent opportunity for a sales\-driven individual to develop expertise in energy brokerage while making a significant impact on the marketplace. **Responsibilities:** * Engage in arranging transactions in energy commodities, focusing on European power \& gas. * Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, including banks, hedge funds, trading houses, and corporates in the European market. * Support the onboarding of new clients and ensure ongoing relationship management. * Assist in client communication, providing regular updates and ensuring satisfaction. * Develop strategies to increase profit and growth by analyzing market trends and products. * Contribute to process improvements and assist in the development of new frameworks. * Collaborate with internal teams, including Operations and Technology, to support trading functions. * Ensure compliance with regulatory standards. * Uphold high standards of integrity and professionalism in line with company values and policies. * Ensure that you are fully aware of and adhere to internal policies that relate to you, your role or any other activities for which you have any level of responsibility * Report any breaches of policy to Compliance and/ or your supervisor as required * Escalate risk events immediately * Provide input to risk management processes, as required. * Experience/knowledge of working in a regulated financial services environment with a good knowledge of the risk and compliance requirements associated with this. ***The Company may require you to carry out other duties from time to time*** **Competencies:** * Resilient in a challenging, fast\-paced environment. * Strategic collaborator with insight and agility, able to anticipate future challenges, ensuring operational effectiveness. * Strong drive and ambition to succeed in a sales\-driven role. * Excellent communication and relationship building skills. * Proactive, resilient, and results\-oriented with the ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Very strong numerical and analytical skills with an interest in market dynamics. * Ability to work autonomously as well as part of a team. * Willingness to learn and grow in the energy trading sector. **Skills and Experience:** * A strong interest in sales and energy markets. * Strong verbal and written communication skills. * Ability to engage and influence clients effectively. * Resilience and the ability to perform under pressure in a dynamic environment. * A basic understanding of financial markets and regulatory requirements (training will be provided). * Ability to speak a European language, in addition to English, would be an advantage. **Conduct Rules** You must: * Act with integrity * Act with due skill, care and diligence * Be open and cooperative with the FCA, the PRA and other regulators * Pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly * Observe proper standard of market conduct * Act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers **Company Values** Acting as a role model for the values of the Company: **Be collaborative** \- by working together across the organisation, we foster teamwork, can better respond to challenges and successfully deliver for our clients **Act with integrity** \- we pride ourselves on our honesty and high ethical standards. We apply these values when working with all our clients, colleagues and other stakeholders **Be adaptable and entrepreneurial** \- we embrace change as markets evolve to constantly increase our efficiency and create innovative solutions for our clients. We are interested in the world around us and inquisitive about understanding the challenges and opportunities our clients face. **Be respectful** – how we treat each other, and our clients says everything about who we are. We always act respectfully and treat people fairly in everything we do. **Nurture talent** – we aim to grow our own talent and make Marex the place ambitious, hardworking and talented people choose to build their career. This means giving and taking stretch opportunities, taking risks, and committing to career development and support – for ourselves and our teams. Marex is fully committed to being an inclusive employer and providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment process for all. We will provide reasonable adjustments to remove any disadvantage to you being considered for this role. We value the differences that a diverse workforce brings to the company. We welcome applications from candidates returning to the workforce. Also, Marex is committed to avoiding circumstances in which the appearance or possibility of conflicts of interest may exist within the hiring process. If you would like to receive any information in a different way or would like us to do anything differently to help you, please include it in your application.

title: Junior Marketing Executive
description: ##### **Bray, Co. Wicklow.** We are excited to announce an opening for a Junior Marketing Executive within our team! This is a crucial role in ensuring seamless operations within Softworks. This individual must be highly motivated and detail\-oriented, as the successful candidate will be responsible for content creation, events, and marketing operations. This is an excellent opportunity for a proactive and creative individual to contribute to the success of a growing organisation and develop their marketing skills while gaining hands\-on experience. #### **Role Responsibilities:** #### **Video Production \& Content Creation:** * Create and edit video content for social media, marketing campaigns, events, and internal projects. * Record and produce video content from initial concept through to final editing and delivery. * Ensure all content aligns with brand guidelines and marketing objectives. #### **Market Research \& Insights:** * Monitor market trends, competitor activity, and industry developments to identify opportunities and inform marketing strategies. #### **CRM \& Data Management:** * Support data enrichment and database maintenance activities. * Perform data cleansing and data upload tasks to ensure CRM accuracy and integrity. #### **Sales \& Event Support:** * Assist with sales enablement activities for events, including preparing materials, presentations, and resources. * Support event delivery, evaluations, and post\-event reporting. #### **Administrative \& Marketing Support:** * Provide general marketing and administrative support across the department as required. * Assist with ad\-hoc projects and initiatives to support broader business objectives. #### **Continuous Improvement:** * Foster collaboration within the Softworks Group and liaise effectively with other departments and stakeholders. * Maintain compliance with international data privacy laws (e.g., GDPR). * Stay updated with the latest industry trends and best practices, particularly digital marketing trends, and AI innovations relevant to global markets. #### **Requirements:** #### **Education \& Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing or related field #### **Professional Skills:** * **Demonstrated interest in marketing, digital content creation, and event management** * Confidence in creating and producing video content, from filming through to editing and final delivery * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the confidence to engage professionally with customers and stakeholders where required * Willingness and flexibility to travel for events and business activities as required * Excellent organisational skills with a high level of attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities * Experience using video editing software and content creation tools * Proficiency in Microsoft office and Google Workspace (G\-suite) applications #### **Expectations:** * Demonstrate agility in a fast\-changing technological environment, being open and adaptable to unsuspecting change. * Encouraging excellence and goal achievement within Softworks, while striving for the same in your personal development. * Communicate regularly and efficiently with all stakeholders, both internal and external. * Maintain strong co\-operative relationships within the team and other departments to ensure that the organisation continues to function successfully. * Ability to analyse complex data, identify issues, and recommend solutions. * High level of accuracy and a commitment to producing high quality work. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present information clearly and concisely. * Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and meet deadlines. * Understanding of business operations and how marketing supports strategic objectives. * Strong ethical standards and a professional approach to work. #### **About the Softworks Group:** For over 30 years, we have been assisting companies around the world in organising the working day to make them more productive, profitable, and add value to their operations. Our globally recognised and acclaimed Workforce Management Solutions include: * Time \& Attendance/Flexible Working * Labour Scheduling \& Optimisation * Absence Management * Project Tracking * Access Control We take a customer\-centric approach to always exceed expectations and delight our clients with a standout and powerful product. Within the Softworks Group, we are more than just people that work together; we belong to a team, all working towards one common goal – to create, market, and provide unparalleled support for the world’s most advanced and effective Workforce Management Solutions. #### **Location:** The role will be based in Softworks HQ in Bray, Co. Wicklow. We currently have hybrid working in place, combining office\-based working with working from home. #### **Benefits:** * Annual Leave starting at 23\.5 days * Flexible Schedule * Hybrid Working * Workplace Initiatives to Promote Employee Wellbeing * Vibrant Social Club * Ministry of Fun * Regular Office Lunches * Cycle to Work Scheme * Pension Contributions #### **Salary Details:** €28,000 – €32,000 DOE #### **How to Apply?** Send your CV and Cover Letter to careers@softworks.com Agency assistance is not currently required. #### ***Softworks is an equal opportunity employer.***

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: At JJ Rhatigan, we have been delivering excellence in construction for over 70 years. As one of Ireland's leading building contractors, we are committed to innovation, quality, sustainability, and developing talented people who want to build rewarding careers. We are currently seeking a motivated and ambitious **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our commercial team in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or an early\-career Quantity Surveyor to gain hands on experience working on some of Ireland's most prestigious construction projects while learning from experienced commercial professionals. The Role Reporting to the Commercial Manager, Managing Quantity Surveyor, or Senior Quantity Surveyor, you will support the commercial management of projects throughout their lifecycle. You will gain exposure to procurement, subcontractor management, cost reporting, valuations, contract administration, and commercial controls while contributing to the successful delivery of major construction projects. Key Responsibilities Procurement \& Tendering * Assist with take\-offs and tender documentation. * Issue tender enquiries and track tender returns. * Review quotations against drawings, specifications, and scope requirements. * Prepare tender comparisons and identify commercial risks, exclusions, and scope gaps. * Support communication with subcontractors and suppliers throughout the procurement process. Commercial Administration * Assist in preparing monthly applications and valuations. * Support the assessment of subcontractor and consultant payment applications. * Assist with payment processing through SAP. * Maintain accurate commercial records and documentation. Reporting \& Cost Control * Support the preparation of monthly commercial reports, CVRs, cashflows, and project reviews. * Assist in monitoring project costs and forecasting financial performance. * Contribute to maintaining commercial procedures and reporting deadlines. Contract \& Document Management * Maintain commercial records and project documentation on SharePoint. * Support subcontractor appointment processes and ensure documentation is complete before work commences. * Assist with variation tracking and commercial correspondence. Commercial Support * Assist with value engineering initiatives and cost\-saving opportunities. * Support dispute resolution and claims preparation when required. * Provide estimating support during tender periods as needed. What We're Looking For **Essential** * Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related discipline. * Strong numerical and analytical skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team and manage multiple priorities. Desirable * Previous placement, internship, or work experience within the construction industry. * Knowledge of construction contracts and commercial processes. * Experience using SAP or similar commercial systems. What We Offer * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Structured career development and mentoring. * Exposure to large\-scale, high\-profile construction projects. * Ongoing professional development and training opportunities. * Support towards achieving professional accreditation. * Opportunity to grow your career with one of Ireland's leading construction companies. If you are looking to build a successful career in quantity surveying with a company that values excellence, collaboration, and professional growth, we would love to hear from you. **Apply today and become part of the JJ Rhatigan team.**

title: Junior Web Developer
description: We are looking for a Junior Web Developer to join our team \- this is an excellent opportunity to push your web development skills to the next level as part of an award winning digital marketing agency. Wolfgang Digital is one of Ireland’s and Europe’s most decorated performance digital marketing agencies, winning Best Large Integrated Agency 2 years in a row at the Global Search Awards. Our mission is to continually deliver exceptional digital marketing that drives extraordinary business growth for our 100\+ clients. Our culture is one of ongoing learning and development. We invest a lot of time each month in upskilling \& development to ensure our team’s technical \& communication skills are constantly improving. **NB** **This is a hybrid role. We work in our office in Dublin city centre on Wednesdays, Thursdays \& Fridays. The rest of the week you decide where you work, in the office or remotely.** **The Role:** * Junior Web Developer * Manage relationships and communication with both colleagues and clients * Working closely with senior colleagues to develop business intelligence applications such as time management and pipeline management * Liaise with our SEO, Social, Content, Creative, Paid Search, CRM \& Marketing Automation and internal teams as and when needed for relevant dev work. **The Candidate:** * A bachelor's degree in information technology, computer science, or a similar field. (or final year) * A portfolio of web and app design and projects you've worked on * Good planning, analytical, and decision\-making skills * Proven working experience in web programming * A solid understanding of how web applications work including security, session management, and best development practices * Adequate knowledge of relational database systems and web application development * Ability to work and thrive in a fast\-paced environment, learn rapidly and master diverse web technologies and techniques. * Experience with Next.js and React * Proficiency in TypeScript * Experience with Supabase (or similar PostgreSQL\-based backend\-as\-a\-service platforms) * Experience with API's * Experience with Git workflows and version control **Desirables:** * SEO experience (under\-the\-hood aspects of a website that can affect crawling, UX, website structure, website speed, robots.txt, htaccess) * Knowledge of Google Tag Manager, Google Analytics, Shopify, Unbounce, and Wordpress CMS * Experience working with HTML, CSS and Javascript * Solid understanding of web design and development principles. * Experience with AWS (Amazon Web Services) or similar (Google Cloud, etc.) * Integrating data from various back\-end services and databases * Accomplished user of Google Analytics * Interest in using AI tools to enhance development workflow * Familiarity with AI coding assistants **Wolfgang Digital is:** * We are the First Irish Company to Transition to Employee Owned * After 1 year with us, you’ll be eligible for Employee Ownership Profit share * Certified Great Place to Work (Ranked \#7 Best Workplaces In Ireland™ 2026 \- Mid\-Size) * Paying a market\-rate salary with the opportunity for performance bonuses * Bike to Work Scheme and Tax Saver Travel Schemes available * Wellbeing perks including massages, counselling, and medical checks * An inclusive, equitable and diverse workplace * Hybrid work model (we are in the office three days a week) * Flexi\-Time * Training and development opportunities * Continuous professional development through the Wolfgang Academy * Team social events every quarter * Annual tree planting events with Wolfgang Reforest * Big on winning awards \- you'll be challenged to become World\-Leading (Our Awards)

title: Kitchen Assistant/Commis Chef
description: Job Description: Kitchen Porter / Commis Chef Gastro Pub Community‑Focused, We are a busy, welcoming gastro pub known for good food, warm service, and a strong sense of community. Our kitchen team takes pride in producing high‑quality dishes using fresh ingredients, and we’re looking for someone who wants to be part of that standard. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys a fast‑paced environment, values teamwork, and wants to grow their skills in a professional kitchen. Role Overview The Kitchen Porter / Commis Chef supports the smooth running of the kitchen by maintaining exceptional cleanliness and assisting with food preparation. Key Responsibilities Kitchen Hygiene \& Organisation Keep all kitchen areas clean, tidy, and safe throughout serviceWash pots, pans, utensils, and crockery quickly and efficiently Maintain cleaning schedules and uphold HACCP standards Assist with waste disposal, recycling, and general kitchen organisation Ensure equipment and storage areas are sanitised and well maintained Food Preparation \& Service Support * Assist chefs with basic prep: chopping, peeling, portioning, and plating * Help prepare ingredients for daily service and specials * Follow recipes and instructions with accuracy and care * Support stock rotation, labelling, and correct food storage * Maintain high standards of presentation and consistency in every dish Teamwork \& Communication * Work closely with the kitchen team to ensure smooth, efficient service * Communicate clearly and respectfully with chefs and front‑of‑house staff Show initiative, reliability, and a willingness to learn Contribute to a positive, supportive kitchen culture What We’re Looking For Strong work ethic and ability to stay calm under pressure * Good attention to detail and pride in maintaining high standards * Positive attitude and eagerness to develop culinary skills * Ability to follow direction and work well within a team * Commitment to cleanliness, safety, and consistency Experience * Previous kitchen experience is helpful but not essential * Full training provided for the right candidate What We Offer * A friendly, supportive team environment * Opportunities to learn and progress within the kitchen * Staff meals and in‑house benefits * Flexible hours depending on business needs * A chance to be part of a respected local gastro pub with a strong community Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €14\.00\-€16\.00 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: L3 Infrastructure Engineer
description: This isn't a heads\-down ticket\-closing role. We need someone who can run complex infrastructure projects end\-to\-end, manage major incidents with calm authority, and explain technical decisions clearly to a non\-technical senior leadership team. If you've been the person your team turns to when things get hard, this is the role for you. **What you'll own** * **Change management** Own the full CAB process. Coordinate vendors and stakeholders, present changes in plain English to a non\-technical SMT, and produce clear post\-change documentation every time. * **P1 / major incident management** Lead major incidents from detection to resolution. Chase vendors, keep leadership informed at every step, and write up root cause analysis reports to close out properly * **Infrastructure project delivery** Take projects from design to go\-live. You won't be handed a single piece of someone else's plan — you'll own the whole thing and coordinate everyone around it * **Platform ownership** VMware vSphere (independent builds and upgrades), Veeam backup and DR, Windows Server 2016–2022, Azure / M365, and firewall management across the estate **What we're looking for** * 4\+ years in a corporate infrastructure environment with demonstrable project leadership * Hands\-on VMware vSphere: not just certification awareness, real build and upgrade ownership * Veeam backup and DR: configured and managed independently * Strong Windows Server (2016–2022\), Azure / M365, and firewall management experience * Compliance framework awareness: ISO 27001, SOX, NIST * ITIL\-aligned thinking: especially around change and incident management * Clear, confident communicator who can talk to both a NOC engineer and a CFO without changing what they're saying, just how they say it * Financial services or MSP background

title: Linen Porter
description: You will receive training through our Dalata Academy to help you learn important skills. You will also meet experienced team members who can help you grow. At Dalata, we believe in promoting from within. If you work hard, you can have new opportunities in the future. Join us and be part of a friendly team! **Key Duties And Responsibilities** * Supplying linen and guest supplies to all Accommodation Assistants * Carrying out regular stocktakes of linen and guest supplies * Ensuring the upkeep and cleanliness of public areas and the linen room * Always providing excellent customer service to guests **Requirements** * Experience working with customers * An excellent command of the English language * Excellent organisational skills * Experience working in a fast\-paced environment * Be a strong team player **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off F\&B in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels * 30% off the Friends \& Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – We bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. We see our growth and expansion plans as a way of spreading our unique way of caring for our guests further around the world, bringing better experiences to more people. Currently, the Dalata difference can be felt at each of our hotels in Ireland, the UK, Germany \& the Netherlands, and we’ll keep expanding this further, focusing on key UK cities, Dublin and Continental Europe. Helping our people to grow and develop their careers is vitally important to us and is one of our highest priorities. We have many exciting and unique internal development programmes, each crafted to make sure our employees get the learning they need to be able to take the next step in their careers. Dalata Hotel Group Plc is an Inclusive Employer. We actively encourage people from all backgrounds and abilities to apply to be part of our team. However, we understand everyone's needs are different; therefore, if you require any accommodations with this application process, please highlight your needs when you are completing your application process.

title: MECHANICAL & ELECTRICAL DESIGN ENGINEERS -BUILDING SERVICES
description: Senior \| Intermediate \| Junior You will work on high\-quality projects as part of a collaborative and professional environment, contributing to the delivery of building services design from concept through to completion. Key Responsibilities * Deliver electrical/mechanical building services designs across all project stages * Design systems including power, lighting, heating, ventilation and associated services * Coordinate with multidisciplinary project teams * Support project delivery to a high technical standard * Engage with clients and stakeholders (senior level) * Mentor and support junior engineers (senior level) * Take increasing ownership of project elements (intermediate level) Requirements Senior Engineers * Degree in Electrical/Mechanical Building Services Engineering * Strong experience within an Irish building services consultancy * Proven project delivery and client\-facing experience * Strong technical and coordination skills * Ability to lead projects and mentor engineers Intermediate Engineers * 3–6 years’ experience in building services design * Consultancy experience * Strong technical and coordination capability * Motivated to progress to senior level Junior Engineers * Degree in Electrical/Mechanical Building Services Engineering * Strong interest in building services design * Good communication and teamwork skills * Willingness to learn and develop Development \& Salary Junior Engineers You will be supported in your professional development, with structured guidance and real project experience to help you build a long\-term career within PMEP. Salary will be competitive and reflective of your qualifications and potential, with clear progression as your experience develops. Intermediate Engineers You will be supported in your continued professional development, with increasing responsibility and project ownership to help you progress your career within PMEP.
