Senior Engineers You will take ownership of project delivery and play a key role in maintaining technical standards, mentoring engineers and supporting the continued growth of the team, with opportunities to further progress your career within PMEP. Salary will be competitive and reflective of your experience, technical capability and level of responsibility.

title: MERCHANDISER - ROI
description: LEARN MORE ABOUT US AND THE ROLE:**Location:** Knocklyon, Dublin (D16 P6EK, A96 P2R9\) **Hours:** 2 hours per week **Days:** Monday **Benefits:** Holiday Entitlement, Merchandiser Recognition Programme, Employee Benefits Scheme, Expenses/Mileage/Travel Time where applicable As an inclusive employer Carlton Greetings are passionate about creating a workforce that represents the diversity of our communities and the customers we supply. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for this flexible and customer focused role where every day offers fresh challenges; from recognising new sales/merchandising opportunities, to displaying brand new ranges. As a part time Greeting Card Merchandiser you will be the face of Carlton Greetings, calling upon retail outlets in and around your local area, ensuring our Customers’ displays are of the highest standard. **The role involves:** * Visiting local retailers, high street chains or supermarkets * Tidying \& filling greeting card \& gift dressing displays * Locating \& unpacking deliveries for replenishment of displays * Installation of new displays * No cold calling or direct selling is involved **To be a Part Time Merchandiser, you will need to have:** * Reliable \& conscientious work ethic * Flexibility around working times \& notably during peak seasonal periods when overtime is likely to be required * The initiative \& ability to work on your own * A strong team ethic * A confident \& friendly personality * No experience is required, as we offer full training * Full driving licence (desirable) So if you love to get out and about, enjoy meeting new people and would like to be part of a great team that promotes and values diversity, then this may be the role for you! WHO WE ARE At Carlton Greetings we are passionate about creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity. We help make the world a more thoughtful and caring place every single day with our greeting cards, gift dressings and social expressions products created for life’s big events, special occasions and those “saw\-this\-and\-thought\-of\-you” moments of life. Along with our sister Company John Sands, we’re part of the American Greetings Corporation, one of the largest privately owned greeting card publishers in the world. We’re one of the largest direct to retail publisher of greeting cards and social expression products in the UK \- so you’ll find our amazing products just about everywhere! **Here at Carlton Greetings we take pride in being an inclusive and diverse employer \- we** **are dedicated and committed to building a culture where difference is valued and** **everyone is able to** **fulfil** **their potential at work regardless of their background.**

title: Management Trainee Hospitality (m/f/d) Dublin
description: **Our heart beats turquoise.** Working at Motel One is like coming home to friends, where you can be your true self and where appreciation and support matter. We meet on an equal footing and see ourselves as one team: from our management to our trainees. We create a home away from home for our guests with our shared passion for modern design and high\-quality service. Our more than 100 hotels in 13 countries impress with exclusive design, high service standards and first\-class inner\-city locations at an attractive price. As the founder and pioneer of the budget design concept, we are successfully expanding in the metropolises of Europe and beyond. Become part of our team and develop Motel One with us. **Job Description** Grow with us! You’re not looking for just any job after graduation? You want to shape your future, learn, and take on responsibility as a future Hotel Manager (m/f/d) – while bringing your passion for hospitality into the role? Then our Management Trainee Program is the perfect stepping stone for a career with long\-term perspective at the Motel One Group. To optimally prepare you for your future role as Hotel Manager, you will rotate between 3 hotels (national and international) over 12–18 months, gaining hands\-on experience in all operational departments. Additional projects and guidance from experienced mentors will help you develop your full potential and expand your expertise. What to expect – Your path to Hotel Management: * **Training\-on\-the\-Job:** You will rotate through all operational departments – from Front Office to Housekeeping to F\&B Service – and learn what true hospitality means. * **Learning by Doing:** You will actively contribute to daily operations, identify opportunities for improvement, and help shape the guest experience. * **Leadership Development:** You will gain insight into leadership roles, support and observe managers, assist with team coordination and motivation, and take on your own leadership projects. * **Mentoring \& Feedback:** A mentor will support you throughout the entire program and beyond. A personal development plan and regular feedback sessions will help you grow. * **Training \& Networking:** You will attend internal training sessions and workshops at our Motel One Campus, build your professional network, and prepare for your future leadership role in the hotel industry. **Qualifications** What you bring: * A completed degree in Hospitality / Tourism / Business Administration or a comparable diploma from a tourism school. * At least 1 year of operational experience in various hotel department; initial leadership experience preferred. * Flexibility regarding location and willingness to travel. * You want to take on responsibility, lead by example, motivate your team, and make bold decisions. Driven by your passion for hospitality and your ambition to become a Hotel Manager you are ready for growing into a leadership role. * Strong social skills, an open and communicative personality, and a positive attitude. * Analytical thinking, strong quality awareness, and a high level of service orientation. * A structured, independent, and team\-oriented working style – because we achieve our goals together! What we offer: * Upon successful completion of the program, you will be promoted to a leadership position. * A competitive salary, housing allowance for rotation assignments, and coverage of travel and accommodation expenses to the Motel One Campus in Munich. * Attractive benefits offered by the Motel One Group – more information available on our careers page. Are you ready for improving the world of hospitality? Then apply now for our Management Trainee Program and kick\-start your career with the Motel One Group!

title: Market Data Business Analyst
description: **Overview** **What we do** We are experts in trading essentially all listed financial products and asset classes, with a focus on derivatives trading. Through market making and market taking, we handle millions of trading transactions around the world every day, providing liquidity and ensuring competitive prices for buyers and sellers. While our presence in the market is broad, our trading desks are highly specialized, allowing for a deep understanding of unique drivers of each asset class. **Job Summary** We are currently seeking a Market Data Business Analyst to work in a growing, diverse and challenging real\-time market data environment. The successful candidate will provide expertise in vendor relationship management, cost management, market data compliance and analysis and change management. They will lead projects related to Market Data governance through the business to successful conclusions and ensure best practice policy and procedure is being followed at all times. The candidate will represent Susquehanna in meetings of Market Data user groups such as iPug and ensure Susquehanna is utilising all available industry standards and knowledge to minimise exposure to Market Data costs. It is essential that the candidate has a proven track record working in the financial services industry specifically in the Enterprise Market Data domain. They should have experience with Market Data entitlements system(s) e.g. DACS or DART, dealing with exchanges and vendors in the market data space. The successful candidate will be able to analyse the existing cost base for Market Data spend and look for opportunities to reduce it by sourcing feeds from a variety of providers. The candidate must have extensive knowledge and experience of exchange specific data feeds, and be able to understand and map this to business specific data content and performance requirements. The candidate should also have a good understanding of the trading environment, rules and methodology in use by the major exchanges across Europe and other major global exchanges. They will work as part of a team to respond to audits carried out by Exchanges or 3rd parties, report to senior managers on the outcomes and take any necessary actions resulting from them. The role will involve working closely with market data engineering, infrastructure engineering teams, application developers, and business users to proactively enhance the market data stability and optimise business performance. The successful candidate will work with colleagues in the US and Sydney offices to ensure homogenisation of global policies and practices where these are possible. The candidate will liaise regularly with the business and technology teams to provide market data subject matter expertise. Good interpersonal, communication and task management skills are a must and the candidate must be able to work off his/her own initiative to ensure the market data objectives of the business are met to an agreed expected standard. **Responsibilities Include** * Liaise with business and technology users to understand current and desired market data usage requirements. This may often involve seeking and establishing new business relationships. In these cases, liaise with the business user and relevant internal legal and technology teams to confirm the suitability of the data and intended provisioning, and thereafter to coordinate data procurement. * Proactively analyse market data announcements, assessing the relevance and impact to our current market data production environment, highlighting impact to the relevant technology and business teams. * Market Data Cost Control Management specifically driving market data cost efficiency initiatives and cost management reporting to senior Management. * Market Data Exchange Audit Management * Internal Susquehanna Trading Application Market Data Controls Audit Management * Vendor Management and point of escalation for Internal Susquehanna Market Data customers. * Manage on\-going relationships with vendors, to understand impact and prerequisites of Exchange driven changes, work with pre\-sales on new Enterprise solutions, and escalate any production or cost impacting issues * Build strong relationship with application teams and business leads to understand fine detail around normalized Enterprise data requirements and supply required enhancements where possible * Development of inventory, financial and contractual controls for all market data services including report generation for exchanges and management **What We're Looking For** * 8\+ years’ experience in financial markets and applications. Exposure to working with real time market data exchange feeds in a low latency environment is an advantage * Strong Market Data Experience and Domain knowledge is essential. * Experience in NYSE Technologies market data platform, Bloomberg (terminals, Data License, B\-pipe) and Refinitive RMDS software concepts, configuration and understanding of their inner workings * Enthusiastic, hardworking, and detail oriented, with the ability to think creatively and to communicate with confidence and clarity at all levels * Exposure to Project Management is an advantage. *If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.*

title: Middle Office Operations Associate - Private Credit
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. **Position Summary** KKR is seeking a Middle Office Operations professional for its Global Investment Operations Team based in the Dublin office. This individual will work and collaborate with the global operations team, as well as employees in other functions and businesses at the firm. This role is a unique opportunity to become part of an evolving and strategic function dedicated to ‘best\-in\-class’ operations management practices to support our global business growth. **Responsibilities** * Preparation and review of NAV and regulatory reporting * Supervision of daily cash and position reconciliations * Investigate and resolve discrepancies between internal systems and trustees/custodians * Manage external service providers * Record and maintain all lifecycle transactions, capital activity, and borrowings in internal systems * Deliver timely, accurate support to external clients and internal stakeholders * Undertake ad hoc project work, **Ideal Experience** A successful candidate will have relevant experience in fund accounting, fund administration or coming from an operations background (credit/asset management, private equity/infrastructure fund industry experience is preferred). Strong knowledge of NAV calculation on fund vehicles and/or Fund of Fund experience would be a distinct advantage. **CRITICAL COMPETENCIES FOR SUCCESS** * Clear intellectual curiosity and commercial acumen with the drive and potential to succeed in a fast\-paced work environment * Self\-starter – someone who enjoys “rolling up his/her sleeves and getting things done”; has high energy; a strong work ethic; displays the ability to work independently; be creative; and entrepreneurial * Grasps key concepts quickly and displays potential to develop greater specialist knowledge * Solid interpersonal and relationship\-building skills – can effectively develop trusted relationships with many constituents, build credibility quickly, and partner effectively with others * Strong execution and project management skills – highly organized, very detail oriented, proactive problem\-solver, solutions\-oriented, and a knack for anticipating obstacles and addressing issues early on * Efficient and effective work style in a dynamic environment with the ability to multi\-task and work across a number of different work\-streams, assignments and/or projects. Proven ability to manage heavy workloads while remaining attentive to details, and prioritizing time efficiently when faced with multiple projects * Focuses on delivering exceptional client service, with a sense of urgency and follow through * Displays maturity, outstanding judgment, critical thinking, sensitivity and creativity to changing needs and situations. Acts professionally regarding sensitive subjects and maintains confidentiality while exercising discretion at all times * Team\-player – willing to pitch\-in when needed to help on projects, tasks and initiatives, as and when needed; enjoys working in a collaborative and collegial environment; humble and enthusiastic with the desire to learn * Strong working knowledge of MS Office, particularly advanced Excel and previous experience with Advent Geneva would be preferable. KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

title: Network Support Engineer
description: Our team is building resilient, secure, and scalable cloud platforms that support the next generation of digital financial applications. We’re looking for a Platform Engineer with hands\-on experience in AWS infrastructure and networks to help build and support our cloud\-native environments. In this role, you will work as a key contributor within our cloud platform team, focusing on automation, infrastructure as code, and secure delivery pipelines. You’ll collaborate with engineering, security, and operations teams to deliver well\-architected solutions aligned with industry best practices. • 7\+ years of experience in software engineering, platform engineering, or site reliability engineering, including 5\+ years of direct experience with AWS. • Proven experience designing production\-grade cloud infrastructure in regulated environments. • Deep understanding of cloud security, networking, identity and access management, and governance within AWS. • Advanced knowledge of networking (firewalls, DNS, Load Balancing, Proxies, etc.) • Proficiency in a scripting or programming language (e.g., Python, Go, Shell). • Experience with continuous integration/delivery pipelines and GitOps workflows. • Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset. • Experience with multi\-account cloud architecture, landing zones, or control tower setups. • Familiarity with zero\-trust architecture, data encryption, and key management systems (KMS). • Strong proficiency with using Infrastructure as Code tools such as Terraform to build cloud infrastructure solutions and automating cloud deployments. • Prior experience in financial services, fintech, or other regulated industries. • Hands\-on experience with containerization (Docker) and orchestration platforms (e.g., EKS/Kubernetes). • Experience with monitoring tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, Splunk, or Dynatrace, as well as AWS native tools • Experience optimizing cost and performance across large\-scale cloud environments. • Contributions to open\-source projects or active participation in cloud engineering communities.

title: Nights - Team Member
description: We're currently recruiting in our Dublin City Centre (North Docklands) Premier Inn Hotel. Working 12 hours per week, paying up to €15\.95 per hour. Nights Team Member – Dublin City Centre (North Docklands) Premier Inn Come and be a Nights Team Member at Premier Inn in Dublin City Centre (North Docklands). As the go\-to for our hotel overnight guests, use your calm personality to ensure their safety and your customer service skills to help them rest easy and get that good night’s sleep with us. Immediate start, no experience needed. **PAY RATE:** Up to €15\.95 per hour **CONTRACT TYPE:** Fixed term contract until 31/08/2026 . Over 18s only. **HOURS:** 12 hours per week **LOCATION:** Premier Inn Dublin City Centre (North Docklands), 22 Sheriff Street Upper, North Dock, Dublin 1, D01 P3F4 **Why you’ll love it here:** **Training and support:** We’ve got a warm welcome and simple administration system to use that will make your job easier from the start PLUS the opportunities to give you a clear career path within our business. **Whitbread Benefits card:** Enjoy up to 60% off Premier Inn rooms and 25% off meals in all Whitbread Restaurants **Pension and saving schemes:** Company pension and save as you earn scheme **Discounts:** Get money off shops, your utility bills, travel, cinema trips, supermarkets and more **Have breakfast on us:** Enjoy a complimentary Premier Inn breakfast for 2 when you join us **What you’ll do:** Join our team at Premier Inn Dublin City Centre (North Docklands) as a Nights Team Member and look after our hotel and guests overnight. Bring your problem\-solving skills, health and safety knowledge and be part of the UK’s leading hospitality business, Whitbread.

title: Occupational Therapist
description: **Occupational Therapist** **Salary €46,000 \-€77,000 per annum.** **Shift and schedule: Full\-time (37\.5 hours per week). Part\-time hours are also considered (minimum 3 days per week).** **Location: Blanchardstown, County Dublin** **Why Work with Evolve Ireland?** Be part of a supportive, purpose\-driven team dedicated to transforming neurodevelopmental care in Ireland and beyond. At Evolve, we offer: * A values\-led culture where clinical excellence, compassion, and staff wellbeing come first. * Continued professional development, clinical supervision, and reflective practice to support your ongoing clinical growth. * Competitive salary based on experience, with annual salary reviews and pension contributions. * Generous leave entitlement, including 28 days annual leave plus bank holidays \- and a day off for your birthday. * Private healthcare to support your physical and mental health * A company laptop and on\-call IT support to enable seamless remote and hybrid working. * Opportunities to contribute beyond clinical work via the Evolve Skills \& Research Academy: training, research \& content development. * Company events and a collaborative, multidisciplinary team passionate about improving outcomes for neurodivergent individuals and families in Ireland. **The Role** At Evolve Psychology Ireland, we equip our clinicians with cutting\-edge tools and support so they can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care to children, young people, and their families. You’ll join a passionate and supportive multidisciplinary team dedicated to making a meaningful difference in the lives of children and young people. Our service specialises in high\-quality, evidence\-based assessments for autism, ADHD, intellectual disability, communication and coordination difficulties under the Assessment of Need pathway, alongside private work. As an Occupational Therapist, you’ll contribute to comprehensive neurodevelopmental assessments, helping ensure timely, accurate diagnoses that lead to appropriate support and improved outcomes for families. **As an Occupational Therapist, you will be responsible for:** * Conducting **face\-to\-face structured observations** as part of the neurodevelopmental Assessment of Need process. * Conducting **remote developmental history interviews** with parents and carers. * Participating collaboratively in **peer MDT diagnostic discussions**, supported by senior clinicians. * Delivering clear, compassionate **feedback** to families. * Producing accurate **clinical reports**, including **diagnostic formulations**, with the help of our smart tools. * Working efficiently to meet service **Key Performance Indicators** (KPIs). * Delivering **clinical supervision** (where applicable). * Able to travel to clinic base(s) to conduct in\-person assessments (hybrid role). **Our Ways of Working** * **Digital workflows and smart report\-writing** reduce admin time and enhance clinical impact. * **A custom\-built electronic health record system,** developed in\-house using market leading research * **Continuously enhancing internal systems**, based on employee feedback to improve efficiency * **Ongoing training and onboarding** to ensure confidence in all tech and systems * **In\-house IT and operational support**, so you're never left troubleshooting alone **Qualifications and Experience** * BSc in Occupational Therapy (or equivalent) with current CORU registration as an Occupational Therapist. * Minimum of 3 years’ experience conducting diagnostic assessments for autism and/or ADHD, or other neurodevelopmental conditions, particularly in children and/or young people. * Strong understanding of differential diagnosis in neurodevelopmental presentations. * In\-depth knowledge of DSM\-5 diagnostic criteria (or equivalent) for autism, ADHD, and intellectual disability. * ·Trained and experienced in the use of diagnostic tools such as ADOS\-2, ADI\-R, etc * Effective time management and caseload organisation skills, with the ability to manage competing demands and meet clinical deadlines * Highly developed written language skills used to produce high\-quality clinical reports * Well\-developed communication skills with children, young people, and their families, ensuring compassionate, clear, and professional interactions throughout the assessment process * Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team and participate in collaborative diagnostic discussions. * Must be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland. **About Evolve Ireland** At Evolve Ireland, we do things differently. Founded in 2014 by Dr Laura Powling, Evolve Psychology began with a bold aim to redefine what clinical and neurodevelopmental psychology means in practice. Laura’s vision was simple yet powerful: to bring care, connection, empathy, and clinical excellence together, with no compromise. What started as a small practice delivering expert witness and neurodevelopmental assessments has grown into an international independent psychology and neurodevelopmental service providing services across the UK, Ireland and beyond. In Ireland, we are nationally and regionally approved by the HSE. We also accept privately funded referrals. We specialise in a number of different pathways which include: * Neurodevelopmental assessments through the HSE Assessment of Need pathway, * Undertaking assessments under the National Autism Framework * Undertaking assessments referred from Community Disability Network Teams (CDNTs) * Providing adult neurodevelopmental assessments * Private neurodevelopmental assessments (online when clinically appropriate and in person) * SpLD (Dyslexia and Dyscalculia) assessments * Specialist individual assessments by Occupational Therapists and Speech and Language Therapists We are clinician founded and clinician led. Our clinicians, where relevant, are proudly registered with CORU or affiliated with the Psychological Society of Ireland and the Department of Health. Globally, we are an award\-winning, multidisciplinary team of over 100 experts, committed to delivering high\-quality expert services. **What sets Evolve Ireland apart?** * Expert leadership: Founded and led by Dr Laura Powling, a Consultant Clinical Psychologist with 20\+ years’ experience across mental health, psychological, forensic and neurodevelopmental services, bringing deep clinical insight and hands\-on leadership to everything we do. * National HSE approved provider: Recognised for the high\-quality services we provide, offering services regionally and nationally across Ireland * Dedicated Clinical Leadership \- Across all of our clinical pathways * Gold Standard Assessments compliant with NICE, PSI and other national and international assessment standards * Access to digital tools to support assessment delivery, including an electronic health record system * Comprehensive CPD and discipline\-specific clinical supervision offerings * We prioritise clinical quality and real impact, not speed or scale at the expense of care. Our assessments are evidence\-based, multidisciplinary, and designed to support the whole person, not just produce a diagnosis. * We offer more than assessment work. Our clinicians can contribute to the Evolve International Skills \& Research Academy, developing training and post\-assessment support for families and educational providers, shaping neuroinclusive content, or collaborating on research, all part of our broader mission to raise awareness and improve support for neurodivergent individuals, educational institutions and workplaces. * We have a strong, values\-led culture. Compassion, clarity, and integrity guide everything we do, from our team interactions to the way we support families. * Multi\-channel employee engagement\- A dedicated, holistic approach to employee engagement and wellbeing. Including online community information forum, team meetings, company events, open feedback forums, management Q\& A sessions, employee recognition and awards. * We’re growing sustainably, with purpose. With new clinics across Ireland targeting areas of most need and hard to reach communities. This includes hybrid and online assessments, where clinically appropriate. We are also a carbon\-neutral company. Pay: €46,000\.00\-€77,000\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Blanchardstown, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you ADOS and/or ADI\-R trained? Experience: * Autism and/or ADHD assessment: 3 years (preferred) Licence/Certification: * CORU Registration (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Office Administrator
description: **About eScribers** Established in 2005 by court reporting professionals, eScribers has grown to become the leader in reporting and transcription services for courts and government agencies across the US and UK \& Ireland. Our company is private equity backed and growing rapidly worldwide, leading the court reporting market in technology innovations and client services. With customers across the US, UK \& Ireland, we currently hold offices in Phoenix, Virginia, Maryland, London, Dublin and Israel. At eScribers we value professionalism, innovative thinking, and collaboration and this is what guides us in all aspects of our day\-to\-day operations. Come be a part of our growth by joining our outstanding team of professionals! **Job Brief** We are seeking an **Office Administrator** to join our amazing team in Ireland! It is a great opportunity to start your career in a growing company with many opportunities to grow and develop. This is a full\-time position where you will enjoy a dynamic work environment. Working days for this position are Monday to Friday; working hours are 12:00pm\-8:30pm, in our Dublin office. **Responsibilities:** As an **Office Administrator,** you will: * Assist with data entry tasks. * Manage office printing tasks. * Coordinate and interact with various stakeholders via email and phone. * E\-filing/docketing transcripts for various courts when needed. * Provide general administrative support to the office * Download and/or upload files into our workflow systems. * Provide support to other teams, as needed. **Requirements:** We are looking for an energetic, detail\-oriented, organized, self\-starting professional with the following: * Minimum one year of prior experience working with general computer programs: Word, Excel, Adobe/PDF, email, and data entry is required. * A good understanding of the internet environment is required. * Proficient in English and excellent writing skills, spelling, punctuation, and English grammar. * Proficient phone skills. * Ability to multitask and work on multiple project deadlines simultaneously. * Eligible to work in Ireland. * Experience in a legal office environment is a plus but not required. Salary range \- 30K\-35K.

title: Operational Risk Associate
description: Pantheon has been at the forefront of private markets investing for more than 40 years, earning a reputation for an innovative approach to investing in secondaries, co\-investments, and primary fund investments, as well as capital formation across commingled funds, evergreen vehicles and customized solutions. Our specialist investment capabilities span multiple strategies across private equity, infrastructure and real assets, and private credit. Through our collaborative and committed culture, we find new ways to solve complex problems together and deliver innovative investment opportunities across private markets. Pantheon currently manages approximately $82\.3 billion in AUM across all its strategies, serving more than 750 institutional and 638 private wealth clients worldwide For further details please visit www.pantheon.com **Purpose of Role** The Operational Risk Associate will be part of the wider Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk function and support the team in further developing and managing operational risks across all business functions. The position will report to the Head of Enterprise/Operational Risk. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Pantheon’s global operational risk frameworks and policies and procedures for adoption by Pantheon Group entities * Support the ISAE 3402/SSAE 16 annual processes including completion of the document, drafting of controls wording and providing external auditor with supporting information. * Support process and control reviews to identify risks and issues in Pantheon’s control environment. Reviews will include identified areas of risk, monitoring programme and new business processes. * Manage Error/Incident reporting process including reporting to management and resolution of any issues. Ensure monitoring procedures are in line with Pantheon’s risk policies. Direct investigation of serious error or breaches and make recommendations for change to management * Co\-ordinate and assist in providing risk oversight and governance across third party supplier relationships of all Pantheon Group entities * Support business continuity process including BCP and testing programme. * Support to manage various risk\-related frameworks such as the data privacy / data protection, vendor risk, Insurance risk, and assist with any client / business related queries * Support and further develop a centralised library of processes, risks and controls in a strategic risk system **Skills And Experience Required** * Degree level education * Experience of working in a risk function ideally in an investment banking, asset management, private equity environment * Understanding of investment management and products * Ability to work on own initiative and in team * Ability to identify when issues should be escalated for further discussion * Controls awareness * Keen problem\-solving skills, ability to offer solutions to issues **This job description is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities, or requirements. You may be required to perform other job\-related duties as reasonably requested by your manager.** **Pantheon is an Equal Opportunities employer, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't perfectly align we'd still encourage you to apply.**

title: Operations Administrator
description: At **ABB** , we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** Service Operations Manager \_\_ ****Job Summary**** In this role, you will work with the Operations Manager to provide general administrative services at the business level and provide back\-up support within the Operations team within Electrification Service (ELSE) division. This role will be a 12 month fixed term contract. **Our Team Dynamics** Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. title: Operations Supervisor
description: * **Duration: 12 Month Fixed term contract** * **Location: Citywest** **The role** This role is to ensure that the welfare of the people, plant and equipment are managed correctly to ensure we deliver a best\-in\-class service to all our customers in a cost\-effective way. The successful applicant will be responsible for the execution of all the activities required to manage the operational responsibilities across Inbound (receiving, put away/replenishment) or Outbound (pick, pack and ship) operations in the Warehouse. Responsible for assisting in the staffing, training, and scheduling of the warehouse team, while maintaining strict adherence to planning hours, warehouse inventory and to achieve productivity goals. **Key Outcomes** * Shift Performance: allocate resources to ensure all key areas are manned sufficiently to deliver the operational performance required by the business. * Provide hands\-on supervision for staff to maximise productivity and accuracy in their job functions. * Manage and drive the hourly targets set in each area and ensure that these are achieved and report on any exceptions to Management. * Effectively utilising Warehouse Management and Control Systems to monitor, manage and troubleshoot shift performance. * Ability to understand warehouse management systems and operating principles to drive operational performance. * Proactive decision making in relation to interpretation of the warehouse performance data. * Maintain a safe work environment and ensure hygiene needs and regulations are adhered to in line with GDP guidelines. * Safety Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to safety regulations and best practices, creating a secure workspace for all warehouse staff. * Ensure Quality Management and Standard Operating Procedures are adhered to at all times. * Ensure that time and attendance records for your team are accurate and up to date on a weekly basis. * Supervise daily processes to ensure all Key Performance Indicator (KPI) metrics are met or exceeded. * Assist in the training and development of the warehouse team. * Monitor and enhance individual performance through clearly defined expectations, training, coaching and mentoring to achieve desired results. * Monitor the performance of the process and the team and report on same. * Take an active part in the development of new processes in the business to drive efficiency and productivity across the site. * Ensure high standards of communication in all interaction in line with Company Dignity and Respect at Work policy. * Ensure smooth and efficient handover of tasks with incoming Operations Supervisor. * Must be available to work weekends. **Key Skills \& Experiences** * You will need experience in a similar position, ideally in FMCG * Proven experience working in a dynamic fast\-paced environment managing large scale diverse teams. * Excellent Leadership and Interpersonal skills and the ability to work at different levels in the organisation, inside and outside of the operations function. * Very strong communication skills to deal on a regular basis with staff at all levels of the organisation. * Proven capability to implement operational improvements to enhance service and productivity. * Experience in coaching and developing teams to deliver superior results. * Staff management, motivation and influencing skills. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Computer skills; with experience and knowledge of Warehouse Management Software, Excel and Word. * Strong data analytical skills and the ability to interpret data. * In depth knowledge of health and safety regulations and best practices **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Part Time Sales Assistant
description: Sports Direct is the UK's No.1 sports retailer, with an unbeatable range of sportswear, footwear, and equipment at prices that keep customers coming back. With 600\+ stores and still growing, we're part of the Frasers Group: a bold, ambitious business known for shaking up the industry and creating real opportunities at every level. If you've got energy, drive, and a love for retail done at pace, you'll fit right in. **The Role: Sales Assistant** This is retail with momentum. As a Sales Assistant at Sports Direct, you're on the front line of one of the UK's busiest store networks, helping customers find what they need, keeping the floor sharp, and powering the team through every trading day. No two shifts look the same, and that's exactly the point. **What's In It For You** * Competitive hourly rate that rewards what you put in * Flexible working to fit around your life * Staff discount across the full Frasers Group family of brands * Career progression in a fast\-growing retail business that promotes from within * A fun, fast\-paced atmosphere with a team that has your back **Job Description** **What You'll Do** * Greet customers, answer questions, and help them find the right product. * Deliver brilliant service at every touchpoint, from the door to the till. * Keep the shop floor tidy, well\-stocked, and visually on point. * Replenish shelves and rails so the floor is always ready for the next customer. * Process transactions quickly and accurately. * Promote current offers and spot smart upsell opportunities. * Work as one team to hit daily sales and service goals. **Qualifications** **What You'll Bring** * A confident, friendly, positive attitude. * Strong communication skills and a customer\-first mindset. * A team\-player mentality that thrives when the pace picks up. * Flexibility and reliability, especially through peak trade. * Motivation to deliver results and take real pride in your work. * Retail or customer service experience is a bonus, but not essential. We'll back you to learn. **Additional Information** **How We Work** **Our Values Shape Every Shift, Every Conversation, Every Decision** * Think Without Limits: Move fast, take bold action, and bring the team with you. * Own It and Back Yourself: Take responsibility, master the basics, and drive results. * Be Relevant: Stay connected to our people, our partners, and the planet. Interest in these roles is high, so we may close the vacancy early. Get your application in soon to avoid missing out. **Apply now** and join a winning team at Sports Direct, where passion meets performance.

title: Partner Designate | Utilities & Energy Sector
description: Senior Associate – Partnership Track **Energy \& Infrastructure** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Start Date:** Q1 2026 **Practice Area:** Energy \& Infrastructure **About The Opportunity** Our client, a leading Irish full\-service law firm with significant market presence, is seeking an ambitious Senior Associate to join their Energy \& Infrastructure team on a clear partnership track. This is a genuine partnership opportunity for an exceptional lawyer ready to take the next step in their career. You will work alongside market\-leading partners on high\-profile mandates while building your own client base and developing the technical expertise and commercial skills essential for partnership. Ireland's energy transition presents unprecedented opportunities for legal professionals in this space. This role offers the platform, mentorship, and client exposure needed to establish yourself as a future leader in energy law. The Role As Senior Associate On Partnership Track, You Will Work On Sophisticated Energy And Infrastructure Matters With Increasing Autonomy And Client Responsibility, Including Renewable Energy \& Energy Transition * Advise on development, financing, and acquisition of renewable energy projects (wind, solar, hydrogen, battery storage) * Support clients navigating Ireland's energy transition and decarbonisation strategies * Draft and negotiate power purchase agreements (PPAs), connection agreements, and offtake arrangements * Advise on regulatory frameworks including renewable energy support schemes and grid connection processes Energy Regulation \& Compliance * Provide strategic advice on energy regulatory matters and CRU compliance * Advise energy companies on licensing requirements and regulatory authorisations * Navigate complex regulatory frameworks for electricity and gas markets * Support clients on energy policy developments and legislative changes Infrastructure \& Project Development * Work on major infrastructure projects across the energy sector * Advise on project development agreements, EPC contracts, and O\&M arrangements * Support financing structures for large\-scale energy and infrastructure projects * Handle land acquisition, planning, and environmental permitting matters Commercial Contracting * Draft and negotiate complex commercial agreements for energy sector clients * Advise on supply agreements, joint ventures, and strategic partnerships * Support M\&A activity in the energy and infrastructure sectors **Essential Requirements** * Qualified Solicitor with 6\-8\+ years PQE, ideally with energy, infrastructure, or commercial/regulatory experience * Strong technical capability in drafting and negotiating complex commercial agreements * Demonstrable interest in energy sector and understanding of energy transition challenges * Client relationship skills with ability to manage client matters independently * Business development appetite – keen to build own network and client base * Partnership ambition – clear desire to progress to partnership within 2\-4 years Ideal Candidate Profile **Technical Excellence** * Strong commercial drafting and negotiation skills * Understanding of energy regulatory frameworks (or strong regulatory background transferable to energy) * Experience with project development, power sector transactions, or infrastructure matters highly desirable * Ability to quickly master complex technical and regulatory concepts **Commercial \& Client Focus** * Practical, solutions\-oriented approach to legal advice * Ability to understand client business objectives and commercial drivers * Experience managing client relationships and running matters independently * Strong project management skills for complex, multi\-party transactions **Business Development Mindset** * Proactive approach to building professional networks * Enthusiasm for contributing to pitches, thought leadership, and sector engagement * Understanding that partnership requires both technical excellence and commercial contribution * Ambition to become recognized voice in Irish energy sector **Leadership Potential** * Ability to mentor and supervise junior lawyers * Contributing team player with collaborative approach * High standards of professional conduct and integrity * Clear communicator with clients, colleagues, and stakeholders
