The successful candidate will accurately document all work within the company control system while maintaining the highest standards of safety, quality, and compliance. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with a keen interest in electronics, engineering, or mechanical assembly who is looking to build a long\-term technical career. Full training will be provided, enabling you to develop hands\-on skills in testing, fault diagnosis, calibration, and repair within a supportive and professional environment. **Major Responsibilities.** * Perform basic repairs on electronic units, including component replacement and restoration of functionality, under the guidance of experienced technicians. * Assist with the setup, testing, and calibration of electronic units in accordance with approved procedures. * Conduct basic testing and fault\-finding activities to identify straightforward issues, escalating more complex faults as required. * Accurately record repair activities, parts usage, test results, and discrepancies within the company management system. * Assemble and install electronic and mechanical components using techniques such as soldering, crimping, fastening, and bonding (full training provided). * Install repaired assemblies into housings, cabinets, or equipment in line with work instructions and technical documentation. * Prepare repair cost quotation worksheets using established processes and guidelines. * Follow maintenance manuals, technical drawings, and repair documentation to support repair and testing activities. * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe working environment while adhering to all quality, safety, and compliance requirements. * Continuously develop technical knowledge and practical skills through on\-the\-job training and mentoring. **Qualifications** EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS * High School Diploma or equivalent qualification * Recognized technical qualification or relevant experience. * Demonstrable experience in an electronic systems repair environment. **Knowledge/Skill Requirements** * Ability to learn quickly and adapt to new processes, tools, and technologies within a highly regulated aerospace environment. * Demonstrated commitment to quality, safety, compliance, and continuous professional development. * Effective communication skills in English, both written and verbal, with the ability to work collaboratively in a multicultural team environment. * Basic understanding of electronic circuits and troubleshooting principles. * Ability to interpret and follow engineering documentation, maintenance manuals, work instructions, and technical procedures. * Familiarity with common electronic test equipment, including multimeters, power supplies, and oscilloscopes, gained through education, training, or practical experience. * Ability to work with small electromechanical components and use hand and power tools safely and effectively. * Willingness to develop skills in testing, calibration, fault diagnosis, and repair through structured training. Effectively communicate with shop supervision, Engineering and Quality departments to solve technical repair problems. **Other Requirements** * Ability to travel up to 10\-20% of the time to domestic and/or international locations. * Must have a working knowledge of Microsoft Office applications. * Acquires job skills and learns company policies and procedures to complete routine tasks. * Works on assignments that are routine in nature, requiring limited judgment. * May receive detailed instructions on work. Candidates must have right to work. Annual Salary plus benefits on offer for this position. Panasonic is committed to adhering to EU employment laws and regulations. We are an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by EU law or company policy. Individuals who meet the qualifications are expected to perform essential job functions with or without reasonable accommodation. Pre\-employment drug testing is required for safety\-sensitive positions or as may be otherwise mandated by contract or EU law. Due to the substantial volume of responses, we will only be able to respond to candidates of interest. All candidates must possess valid authorization to work in the applicable EU countries. We appreciate your interest in Panasonic Corporation of North America. REQ\-154868

title: Research Intern Vacancy
company: Institute of International and European Affairs
description: The Institute of International and European Affairs (IIEA), a leading independent think\-tank based in Dublin, Ireland, is opening a competition for the position of Research Intern to support the IIEA’s research and events during the Irish Presidency of the Council of the EU. This internship would be for a duration of up to 6 months, depending on candidate availability and start date, and would conclude on 31 December 2026\. This internship would be ideal for recent graduates seeking experience in international affairs. Research Interns at the IIEA work as part of the IIEA research team, reporting directly to the Director of Research, and may be asked to assist on any or all of the following tasks: providing research support for briefings, blogs and other written materials; assisting with event planning and organisation; helping to coordinate a working group comprising leading experts from across the IIEA’s range of research areas; and facilitating the IIEA’s relations with key Irish and EU stakeholders, including government departments, EU institutions, NGOs, and universities. The successful candidate(s) will have a unique opportunity to immerse themselves in EU and international policy research; engage with leaders in policy, politics and business; assist in the creation and execution of programmes of research; assist with events featuring some of the highest profile speakers in Ireland and internationally; and build a network of contacts in Irish and European policy circles. The deadline for applications is Friday 26 June 2026\. **Responsibilities** The main responsibilities of this position include: * Tracking and analysing significant developments in specific EU policy areas, EU institutions, and EU politics * Providing research assistance for analytical papers, blogs, and explainers on themes related to EU and international affairs * Liaising, when required, with IIEA members, speakers, and guests * Providing assistance in the organising of programmes of keynote addresses, seminars, roundtable discussions, and workshops with high\-level policymakers and thought leaders as part of the IIEA's events programme **Requirements:** The ideal candidate would have * First\-class or high second\-class honours in a field such as International Relations, Public Policy, European Studies, Economics, Law, Political Economy, Political Science, etc. (Candidates who have not yet received their final results may be asked to provide academic references.) * Good knowledge of Irish politics and EU institutions * Excellent research, writing, editing, and proofing skills * Ability to meet deadlines * Attention to detail, accuracy, and efficiency * Ability to multitask and strong organisational skills * Excellent interpersonal and oral and written communication skills **Application details:** * To apply please send a CV and cover letter with the subject line “Research Intern – Application” to jobs@iiea.com * LinkedIn applications will not be considered * Due to the expected volume of applications, we will only be able to respond to applicants who are selected for interview * Canvassing will result in disqualification * Please note that this role is Dublin\-based and it is expected that the successful candidate would primarily work from the IIEA’s offices at 8 North Great George’s Street, Dublin 1\. **The salary for this internship is €32,000 per annum *pro rata* .**

title: Risk Operations Analyst, Financial Crimes
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Financial Crimes team plays a critical role in ensuring a healthy financial ecosystem and maintaining trust in Stripe's platform. We ensure that prohibited parties cannot utilize our services and identify bad actors engaging in money laundering, terrorist financing, or other financial crimes. As Stripe's user base and global footprint grow dramatically, this team is essential for mitigating risk and upholding regulatory compliance, directly contributing to Stripe's mission of increasing the GDP of the internet safely and responsibly. What you'll do As a Risk Operations Analyst, you'll work to ensure our policies, operations, and workflows align with the needs of our financial partners. You'll be accountable for workflows impacting a variety of cross\-functional internal and external stakeholders to identify and assess regulatory, contractual, and partner risk. Further, you'll influence and advise operational teams in building out scalable operational processes to ensure compliance with service level agreements, routine reporting, and overall risk mitigation. **Responsibilities** * Demonstrate sound judgment and an ability to navigate non\-binary, high\-risk decisions * Adhere closely to process while still constantly questioning assumptions, suggesting improvements, and balancing user experience with compliance requirements * Effectively and clearly communicate with other Stripes, Stripe's users, and Stripe's financial partners * Identify and assess financial crime risks, conduct reviews using data analysis, and monitor for suspicious activity and trends * Facilitate the redesign of business processes including clarifying, identifying issues, and facilitating changes to attain the highest levels of efficiency, control, and compliance * Help build and scale processes across the financial crimes operations ecosystem * Identify gaps in current systems, policies, and strategies, and recommend enhancements and process improvements to mitigate emerging risks through a collaborative approach with stakeholders Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * 8\+ years of experience in risk management, compliance, or financial operations * Understanding of FinCrime red flags, its typologies, and how they can be identified * Experience in developing and implementing process improvements * Demonstrates reasoned logic in decision\-making and judgment, considers external and internal factors, and recommends solutions in complex situations * Effective communication skills with the ability to produce clear, precise, and concise written and presentation materials * Ability to analyze feedback patterns and use these insights to drive process and product improvements * Policy development and regulatory knowledge—proven track record developing compliance policies and procedures for payment processing environments * Risk assessment—experience conducting merchant risk assessments, transaction monitoring, and implementing risk\-based compliance controls * Operational management and scaled operations—experience driving operational outcomes and managing large\-scale operations through vendor partnerships, third\-party integrations, and automated solutions to achieve efficiency and scalability in compliance processes **Preferred Qualifications** * Data analysis tools such as SQL * Experience in transaction monitoring or financial investigations, Sanctions, AML, EDD, KYC, and KYB * Knowledge of payment processing systems and associated risks In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €74,800 \- €112,200\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Risk Oversight Intern
description: **Risk Oversight Intern** **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **Duration: 6 Months** **Role Overview** The Risk Oversight Intern, reporting into the Risk Manager, will receive comprehensive on\-the\-job training while working alongside experienced risk professionals across Liquidity, Credit, Market and P\&L Risk. The Intern will gain hands\-on experience in a dynamic risk management and trading environment at NBC Global Finance. Trading activities include fixed income, equities, repo, stock loan, total return swaps, futures, options and a variety of other derivatives. The Risk Oversight team reports to the CRO and plays a strategic role within NBC Global Finance as part of the Second Line of Defense. The team provides independent oversight of trading activities and material risks to ensure strong governance, robust controls, and alignment with regulatory expectations and NBC Group’s risk appetite. The Risk Oversight Intern will have the opportunity to contribute to meaningful projects, collaborate across different teams, and develop a deep understanding of risk management and trading products. **Main Responsibilities** The Risk Oversight Intern will gain exposure to a broad range of tasks and responsibilities. Your duties may include: * Monitoring and reporting across key risk areas, including Liquidity Risk, Market Risk, Credit Risk and P\&L: Work closely with colleagues across Front Office, Operations and Risk Oversight to monitor exposures and understand day\-to\-day movements. * Collaborating on new developments and enhancements: Work alongside colleagues on projects and support the ongoing enhancement of risk processes and procedures. * Analysis and reconciliations: Assist in identifying key drivers of risk exposure across trading products and strategies, providing Front Office with valuable insights to support trade execution and risk reduction. * Cross\-department collaboration: Work with colleagues across Front Office, Operations, Regulatory, Legal and other departments to build experience and broaden your understanding of the business. **Skills and Experience:** We encourage applications from candidates with diverse skills, experiences, and perspectives who are currently pursuing, or have recently completed, a degree in Business, Risk, Analytics, or a related field. The ideal candidate will possess: * Effective analytical and numerical skills, with a high level of attention to detail and the ability to review numerical information thoroughly and identify key insights. * Ability to stay focused and complete tasks on time, during challenging or time\-sensitive situations. * Confidence to escalate issues promptly and communicate clearly with senior members of the team when concerns are identified. * Proficiency in Excel, including the ability to work accurately with data and support analysis and reporting tasks. * A team player who can handle different tasks and adapt to changing priorities. * A willingness to learn and maintain high professional standards. * Effective communication skills, with the ability to share ideas clearly, both written and verbal. * A positive, solution\-focused attitude and a desire to contribute to the team. * An interest in finance and curiosity about risk management across Liquidity, Credit, Market and P\&L. **About Us:** NBCGF is a direct wholly owned subsidiary of National Bank of Canada (“NBC”). The main strategic purpose of NBCGF is to enable the NBC Group (“NBC Group,” meaning National Bank of Canada and all its subsidiaries) to grow and diversify its presence in Europe while allowing for a natural development of its existing business relationships and the establishment of new ones in the European markets. NBC has over 150 years of history and is currently the 6th largest Canadian bank. Through its branch and subsidiary network, NBC can service clients throughout North America and Europe. NBC’s strategy is to further develop NBCGF as its hub for trading and financial services in Europe. **What NBCGF has to Offer:** Access to competitive working conditions, a wide range of employee benefits, and a dynamic work environment. Open, respectful workplace where all employees can achieve their full potential. NBCGF recognises that the culture of an organisation and the performance of its workforce are not mutually exclusive. Since our inception in 2013, NBCGF has worked tirelessly to ensure our cultural framework aligns not only with the Code of Conduct and Ethics of our parent company, NBC, but with our own environmental and local values. Promoting and supporting diversity in the workplace is an important aspect of good people’s management\- it’s about valuing everyone in the organisation as an individual. We at NBCGF have an effective diversity and inclusion approach concerning our employees. There are no silos in our teams and management strive to add value to our organisation, by contributing to employee well\-being and engagement. NBCGF takes pride in its diverse work force, employing and cultivating professionals from all over the globe. Working at NBCGF can take different forms and it is built around the relationship of trust between the employer and its employees. NBCGF operates a hybrid working model where the employee’s time can be split between the office and a remote location on a schedule which may vary. The model considers customer expectations, organisational needs, and constraints, as well as the employees’ need for flexibility and the nature of the work they perform. What is important is having the right arrangements in place so that you can best perform your role. We're putting people first. We're a bank on a human scale that stands out for its courage, entrepreneurial culture, and passion for people. Our mission is to have a positive impact on people’s lives. We want our recruitment process to be fully accessible. If you require accommodation, feel free to let us know during your first conversations with us. We welcome all candidates! What can you bring to our team?

title: Room Attendant
description: Be Yourself – Be a Bold Team Player – Be Leonardo Once you’re part of the team, you’re part of the Leonardo community of over 55 hotels across the UK \& Ireland! Main Tasks * Offer exceptional guest care at all times * Clean and service guest bedrooms and bathrooms in line with company standards, ensuring rooms are clean, comfortable, safe and secure for our guests * Work in a timely and efficient manner to complete assigned tasks in line with productivity guidelines * Respond to and complete guest requests in a polite and professional manner * Maintain a clean and tidy working environment for the department, including storerooms, trolleys and corridors * Assist with cleanliness of other areas of the hotel as required * Support with cost control and environmental initiatives in the department, including linen rejects, control of consumables and utility consumption * Operate in a safe and orderly manner, promptly reporting any hazards, accidents, losses, or damages to management * Maintain awareness of utility consumption ensuring all equipment, lights, etc. are turned off when not in use * Utilise all relevant systems correctly to complete tasks in a timely manner * Adhere to Company policies, Code of Conduct, procedures and relevant legislation at all times – including, but not limited to, Data Protection and Health and Safety regulations; be empowered to work on your own initiative * Friendly team player with the ability to work effectively and efficiently with others * A confident communicator willing to make suggestions for improvements, and open to feedback from colleagues * Engaged with hotel initiatives to support the local community Measurable Performance Indicators * Demonstrating the Company vision, mission and values when interacting with colleagues and guests * Customer reviews * Achieving agreed KPIs * Fulfilment of the job skills checklist * Customer satisfaction scores e.g. Booking.com * Regular review meetings with line manager Available Ongoing Learning * GROWonline BELONG induction and development courses * Academy Technical skills training * Team Coach * ‘Come Join Us’ Guest Care training * Opportunity to apply for internal development programmes, such as: * Junior Chef Development programme * Graduate programme * Level 2 Apprenticeships in Hospitality NOTE: The learning opportunities listed above are correct at the time of issue and are subject to change. With support on tap, top\-notch practical training and clear progression you’ll be on track for a brilliant future in Hospitality!

title: SCADA Integration Engineer
description: **Job Title: SCADA Integration Engineer** **Role Description** AZOROM has an ongoing contract with ESB and wishes to fill this present position. ESB Renewable Operations (ESB Generation and Trading) is responsible for the Operation and Maintenance of ESB’s On\-shore Wind and Distributed Generation asset portfolio. As part of delivery of the Net Zero by 2040 Strategy it is our ambition that we will generate 70% of our electricity generation from renewable assets by 2030 with 1\.7GW of offshore wind, 3\.5GW of onshore wind and 900MW of solar {dependent on planning and development) and also incorporates 500 MW of renewables enabling flexible generation assets including battery energy storage systems, peaking plants and grid support devices such as synchronous condensers. In order to successfully integrate the current pipeline of assets in development and construction, namely, Solar, remotely operated OCGT and on\-shore Wind, Renewable Operations are seeking to fill a temporary role (12 months) to deliver the integration of Asset Operating System (SCADA/HMI) into the Remote Operations Centre. (ROC). **Key Responsibilities** * Manage the integration of OEM HMI into Remote Operation Centre. * Interface between ESB project delivery team and OEM SCADA provider. Manage/attend meetings and ensure project deadlines are met. * Define points list and manage and commissioning integration of same into ESB second tier SCADA system. * Commissioning of RTU/PLC to control and manage Electrical Balance of Plant at Substation. * Work with OEM SCADA provider to interface with ESB Secure network infrastructure ensuring compliance with ESB Cyber security standards. * Manage integration of CCTV systems on site. * Manage commission and integration of site meters into 2nd Tier SCADA system. * Troubleshoot communications issue on new and existing sites. * Complete and maintain as built drawings for new and existing sites. * Contribute to the design SCADA and telecoms solutions for all new onshore wind and distributed generation assets. * Contribute to the maintenance policy for the SCADA system to achieve best practice availability. * Implement and manage the maintenance policy for key communication equipment including broadband, network communication equipment and ancillary equipment on all sites. * Contribute to the design of the Operation Control requirements (SCADA/Telecoms/Cyber systems) to enable future offshore wind remote operations management at ESB. **Knowledge, Skills and Experience** Essential * Excellent Technical and analytical ability with capability to develop large scale data systems and tools. * Excellent knowledge and experience of communication and SCADA IT systems. * A track record in delivering SCADA Integration projects, with relevant experience in managing projects. * 3rd level qualification in engineering, business or equivalent. * At least 10 years postgraduate experience. Desirable * Experience, knowledge and competence in Operations/Maintenance of Renewable/Power Plant and in Renewable/Power Plant technologies, systems and procedures. **Qualifications** Essential * Relevant 3rd level qualification in an appropriate engineering discipline or equivalent work experience is required. Desirable * Experience, knowledge and competence in Generation Assets Control Systems and in Renewable/Power Plant technologies, systems and procedures. **Base Location** The successful candidate will be based at ESB Head Office in Dublin. It will also be a requirement to travel regularly to sites Ireland as part of asset integration work. The successful candidate must work from the base location or on site a full\-time basis. Office and Sites (50/50\). **Work Input** 8:30 – 16:45, Monday to Friday **Anticipated start date** July 2026 **Duration** 12 months

title: Sales Development Representative
description: **Company Overview** -------------------- Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the \#1 company in e\-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM). **What You'll Do** ------------------ Are you an agile, creative, and collaborative Sales Development Representative looking for your first break within Sales, or a chance to grow your Sales career in the tech industry? We can help. You are the driver of building new business relationships, connecting with potential clients who have expressed interest in Docusign and targeting those you identify as having an opportunity to work with. This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Manager, SDR. **Responsibility** * Achieve your SQO (Sales Qualified Opportunity) quota * Spend about 80% of your time on the phone contacting prospective businesses identified as having real potential as a Docusign customer from an inbound lead flow * Educate potential customers on what Docusign has to offer and gain insight into their business needs * Use targeted questions and your natural rapport building skills to understand the client's business strategy and how we at Docusign can contribute to their success * Provide qualified, informative leads in our CRM database for Account Executives to pursue, and initial liaison scheduling **Job Designation** ------------------- **Hybrid:** Employee divides their time between in\-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: Minimum 2 days per week; may vary by team but will be weekly in\-office expectation) Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law. **What You Bring** ------------------ **Basic** * You have excellent communication and interpersonal skills: You love engaging with new people and companies and thrive on making successful connections using your initiative, introducing companies to a product you are passionate about * A multi\-tasker, you enjoy a busy, challenging role where you can take ownership of your success and contribute to a driven team * Experience with Salesforce CRM, Google Workspace, and other core business applications * Experience adopting new technologies in a fast\-paced, AI\-first environment * Fluency in French and English in required **Preferred** * Track record of success in inbound sales/business development role * Confident and courteous verbal and written communication skills * Excellent organizational skills * Familiarity with AI\-powered sales, research, and productivity tools, including Gong, Glean, Gemini, Notebook LM * Bachelor's degree **Life At Docusign** -------------------- **Working here** Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal. We each have a responsibility to ensure every team member has an equal opportunity to succeed, to be heard, to exchange ideas openly, to build lasting relationships, and to do the work of their life. Best of all, you will be able to feel deep pride in the work you do, because your contribution helps us make the world better than we found it. And for that, you’ll be loved by us, our customers, and the world in which we live. **Accommodation** Docusign is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need such an accommodation, or a religious accommodation, during the application process, please contact us at accommodations@docusign.com. If you experience any issues, concerns, or technical difficulties during the application process please get in touch with our Talent organization at taops@docusign.com for assistance.

title: Sales Executive
description: **About Us** We are part of Lookers Motor Group Limited, which includes Charles Hurst Limited who are one of the leading automotive retail groups in the UK and Republic of Ireland. Across the group, we represent 30\+ manufacturer brands, offering a wide selection of new and used vehicles. We firmly believe that our people are our most valuable asset. We value the commitment they make to the business which is why we are continually working to provide a market\-leading rewards and benefits package. **Audi Centre, Dublin** **Contract type: Permanent, full\-time** **Salary: €70,000\.00 Annual OTE** **Working Hours: Monday \- Friday 9am\-6pm, Sat 9am\-5pm. Day off in week in lieu** Are you passionate about exceptional customer service and ready to take the next step in your motor industry career? Audi Centre Dublin is looking for a **driven and customer\-focused Car Sales Executive** to join our dynamic and supportive team. This is your opportunity to represent one of the world’s most prestigious automotive brands while developing your skills and building a rewarding career. Working closely with our Sales Manager and team of experienced professionals, you’ll receive the guidance and support needed to excel and grow. * Engage with customers in person, by phone, and via email to understand their needs * Recommend vehicles and solutions that fit each customer perfectly * Organise and accompany test drives, showcasing the Audi experience * Negotiate and close sales with confidence and professionalism * Manage all related sales documentation and follow\-up processes **What We’re Looking For** * + Strong communication and interpersonal skills + A proactive, positive attitude with genuine enthusiasm for customer care + Ambition to achieve targets and exceed expectations + Previous experience in automotive or customer\-facing roles is ideal, but we also welcome fresh, motivated talent eager to learn and grow in the motor trade The ideal candidate will have strong communication skills, self\-motivated to crack on with the job and passionate about what they do. You will be expected to hit targets so being ambitious and hard\-working is essential. Previous experience in a similar position and within the motor trade would be preferred but not essential as we are always on the look out for new/fresh talent to join our team. **Benefits Include** * Competitive salaries with clear pay scales as you develop. * Generous annual leave allowance that increases with length of service. * Enhanced maternity leave, adoption leave (6 months full pay), and paternity leave (2 weeks full pay). * Life assurance. **Health \& Wellbeing** * Aviva Care – Digital GP, Second Medical Opinion, Bereavement Support * Private Medical Insurance. * Eye care vouchers * Cyclescheme **Leisure \& Lifestyle** * Discounted gym memberships. * Exclusive shopping discounts. We strongly believe in nurturing and providing our people with specific manufacturer brand and management training to enhance career development opportunities. We have a great team and we celebrate and encourage success at all levels. We are an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to a working environment that is free from discrimination, inclusive, and empowers our people to bring their whole selves to work and reach their full potential. If your application is successful, we will conduct relevant employment checks prior to you commencing employment with us. These will include verifying your recent employment, address, credit history, and a standard criminal record check. For roles that require driving, a driving licence check will also be carried out. All offers of employment are subject to a 6\-month probation period from your start date. *We take our data protection responsibilities very seriously and are committed to upholding and respecting your privacy rights. Information about how we will process your data including the types of data we collect, the purposes for which we use it, who we may share it with and how long we keep it are set out in our privacy notice. It also includes information about your individual privacy rights. Please make sure you read our Privacy Notice so that you understand how we may collect and use your data.*

title: Scrum Master
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has opened up for a Scrum Master to join our team at Davy. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Drive Agile delivery and culture, supporting multi\-disciplinary teams in delivering with adherence to Agile principles and Scrum framework, and coaching team members and wider stakeholders on Agile best practice and mind\-set * Facilitate key Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, stand\-ups, sprint reviews, sprint retros etc.) and proactively identify, track and resolve blockers impacting delivery, working collaboratively across squads, the wider organisation and third parties as required * Partner closely with Product Owners to ensure the backlog is well refined with clear priorities, enable allocation of backlog activities across the squad and provide transparency on deliver progress, risk and outcomes * Champion Agile ways of working, governance, standards and tooling **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: Experience \& Background * 5\+ years' experience as a Scrum Master in software delivery environments, with a strong track record of improving team performance and delivery outcomes * Experience working within cross\-functional teams (engineering, product, UX, operations) in medium to large\-scale organisations * Exposure to Agile transformation initiatives, including introducing or maturing Agile practices across teams or organisations * Comfortable operating in both hands\-on delivery roles and coaching/mentoring capacities * Experience working with scaled Agile frameworks (e.g., SAFe, Spotify model) or multi\-team environments is highly desirable Agile Expertise * Strong practical knowledge of Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid models (Scrumban) * Demonstrated ability to facilitate core Agile ceremonies (Sprint Planning, Daily Stand\-ups, Retrospectives, Reviews) while driving continuous improvement * Proven experience establishing and improving Definition of Ready (DoR) and Definition of Done (DoD) frameworks * Familiarity with metrics\-driven delivery, including velocity, cycle time, and flow efficiency tracking Coaching \& Leadership * Ability to coach and mentor teams, Product Owners, and stakeholders to improve Agile maturity and delivery effectiveness * Strong skills in facilitating collaboration, resolving impediments, and promoting a culture of accountability and continuous improvement * Experience embedding lean principles and efficient workflows into team practices * Comfortable influencing across all levels, including senior stakeholders Technical \& Delivery Practices * Good understanding of modern engineering practices, including CI/CD, automation, and DevOps principles * Awareness of Agile engineering techniques such as BDD, TDD, and feature toggling * Experience supporting teams in delivering high\-quality software in iterative, incremental cycles Tools \& Data * Proficiency with Agile tools such as: * Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, or similar * Reporting and analytics tools for tracking Agile metrics * Ability to use data and insights to drive decision\-making and improve predictability **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. Health and Wellbeing Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. Reward and Recognition We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. Growth and Development Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. Positive Workplace Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. Values and Culture Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Sector Specialist - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The **EIB** , the European Union's bank is seeking to recruit for its Projects Directorate (PJ) – Innovation \& Competitiveness Department (INCO) – Circular Economy \& Sustainable Development Division (CESDEV) \- Innovation Fund Unit (IFU) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, a **Sector Specialist \*.** **This is a full time temporary position at grade 5\.** **The term of this contract will be 3 years.** * internal benchmark Sector Engineer *Panel interviews are foreseen as from July 2026\.* Purpose The successful candidate will provide technical assistance to promoters on innovative, low carbon and energy efficiency technology deployment projects in the energy, industrial and net\-zero mobility sectors that are part of the Innovation Fund Sectors. The candidate will assess, within multi\-disciplinary teams, the technical viability and environmental sustainability of projects in accordance to the relevant EU requirements under the Innovation Fund Project Development Assistance (IF PDA). Specific Post Environment The Innovation Fund (IF) is set up under the revised EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS) Directive. The objective of the IF is to support innovative, low carbon technology demonstration projects in energy intensive industries, renewable energy, energy storage, net\-zero mobility, buildings, carbon capture and use or carbon capture and storage (the “Innovation Fund Sectors”). The European Investment Bank (EIB) supports projects eligible for the IF to improve their technical, financial and operational maturity (through Project Development Assistance, PDA), so they can successfully apply to the IF or other financing schemes. More information on the Innovation Fund is available at the dedicated IF website: Innovation Fund \- Climate Action \- European Commission More information on the EIB involvement in IF is available at the dedicated IF PDA website: Innovation Fund \- Project Development Assistance (eib.org) Operating Network The successful candidate will join the EIB’s Innovation and Competitiveness Department, specifically the Innovation Fund Unit. As a Sector Specialist, the candidate will work closely with colleagues in the Unit, other Divisions and Departments of the Projects Directorate of the Bank, as well as other Directorates (such as Operations, Legal, etc.), to ensure that all duties are performed in line with the IF PDA Contribution Agreement between the EIB and the European Commission. The candidate is also expected to liaise with the European Commission (in particular, DG CLIMA) and with the Commission’s agency CINEA, which are the implementing entities of the Innovation Fund. Accountabilities **The Sector Specialist Will** * Review eligibility of projects for support under the IF PDA Contribution Agreement and assess the feasibility and maturity of projects, to screen them in or out, for future PDA activities * Provide technical and economic assessment as well as advisory services to selected projects in line with the IF PDA objectives and requirements * Prepare Terms of References for the procurement of consultant services in the context of the IF PDA and supervise the implementation of such services * Prepare high quality final PDA reports to be provided to project promoters, in line with the Contribution Agreement with the European Commission and the applicable procedures manual * Propose improvements and updates of internal PDA procedures to increase the efficiency of the PDA activities and service delivery * Track the evolution of the legal and regulatory framework related to the Innovation Fund and sectors covered under the Innovation Fund to ensure application of the most up to date requirements in the IF PDA * Represent the EIB for the Innovation Fund in workshops with the European Commission, Member States and project promoters or other events which may include sharing of good practice, guidelines and methodologies * Produce sector and policy papers and represent the EIB at sectorial events as required **Qualifications** * University degree (minimum an equivalent of a Master’s degree) preferably in STEM disciplines related to (at least) one of the Innovation Fund Sectors * Further training and specialisations in specific sector\-related areas of competence will be an asset * Minimum of 5 years relevant professional experience in industries related to one of the Innovation Fund Sectors * Prior professional experience in managing technology and innovation projects, including project planning, budgeting, and execution, or alternatively to funding environment (e.g., grant application or corporate innovation roles) * Strong knowledge of technologies, markets and industry best practices in (at least) one of the Innovation Fund Sectors. Knowledge of sector\-related international and EU policies and the associated regulatory aspects * Experience in managing technical assistance projects or programmes and in managing and working with external experts and consultants will represents a clear advantage * Experience in developing client and stakeholder relationships would be an advantage, in particular with other EU institutions or government entities * Excellent written and verbal communication skills; capable of drafting concise and insightful materials and presenting findings to technical and non\-technical audiences * Strong teamwork, integrity and analytical thinking, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and interface with diverse stakeholders; motivation for personal development in the job is a must * Fluent in English (written and spoken)\*\*, knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage Competencies **Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here** To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* *level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. *This is an open campaign to consecutively fill open positions. The campaign will remain open until position is filled. Applications will be reviewed in order of receipt.*

title: Security Analyst I
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Cyber security, Information Technology or a relevant field, or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience in a SOC environment or Information Security role. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience with scripting. * Experience working with Artificial Intelligence. **About the job** ----------------- As an Associate Security Analyst in the Mandiant Threat Defense (MTD) SOC, you are the frontline defense against evil. You will move beyond simple alert validation to perform deep\-dive investigations. You will leverage Google SecOps and Mandiant’s frontline intelligence to identify novel attacks from infected USB drives to job opportunity phishing campaigns by nation state threat actors. You are a cyber security enthusiast, part of an incredible team, who will constantly strive to improve execution, automate repetitive tasks, and change the way we do security. Part of Google Cloud, Mandiant is a recognized leader in dynamic cyber defense, threat intelligence and incident response services. Mandiant's cybersecurity expertise has earned the trust of security professionals and company executives around the world. Our unique combination of renowned frontline experience responding to some of the most complex breaches, nation\-state grade threat intelligence, machine intelligence, and the industry's best security validation ensures that Mandiant knows more about today's advanced threats than anyone. Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €68000 \- €70000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Analyze real\-time security events across end\-point, network, and cloud environments using a centralized analyst console and SIEM/Google SecOps platform. * Conduct host and network forensic analysis to support incident response efforts, understanding attacker activity, and assessing customer impact. * Execute basic static and dynamic analysis of suspicious files to determine capabilities (e.g., identifying GOOTLOADER JavaScript or CORNFLAKE.V3 backdoors). * Determine the severity, impact, and scope of security incidents and compromises. * Isolate compromised hosts and stop lateral movement or ransomware propagation. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Security Systems Project Engineer
description: **Security Systems Project Engineer \- Dublin** Own the whole job. Survey it. Design it. Commission it. Support it. This is the role for someone who's outgrown purely technical work but doesn't want to lose the hands\-on edge. €60,000 \- €80,000 DOE Dublin\-based · Permanent · Full\-time Not a PM who can't touch the kit. Not an engineer who disappears after handover. The whole lifecycle \- owned by one person. **What you'll do** * Lead projects from survey and risk assessment through to commissioning and handover * Programme and integrate access control, CCTV, and intruder systems at enterprise sites * Diagnose and resolve complex faults \- own the fix, not just the escalation * Manage contractors, materials, and stakeholder comms across every phase * Be the technical point of contact clients from IT to procurement want on site **What we need** * Background in electronic security systems \- access control, CCTV, intruder * Track record owning project delivery, not just contributing to it * Strong client\-facing skills and solid documentation discipline * PSA licence or willingness to obtain one * Full, clean driving licence

title: SharePoint Infrastructure Engineer (12-month Contract)
description: **Role** : SharePoint Infrastructure Engineer **Department** : Modern Workplace **Travel Required** : Flexible Hybrid in Ireland **Contract Type** : 12\-month Contract for Services (Contractor) **About Us:** Storm Technology specialises in helping clients expand the possibilities of what digital technology can do for their business. A Microsoft Solutions Partner with over 27 years’ experience, Storm Technology is the solutions partner of choice for digital technology advancement and adoption. Our team is comprised of highly skilled business professionals, technology experts and change management specialists, who bring business and technology know how together with a proven project methodology to deliver tangible return on investment. They are passionate individuals who thrive when part of a team which designs the best technology solutions for our clients and are driven by our client’s success. **Contract overview** : We are seeking a SharePoint Infrastructure Engineer experienced in SharePoint on\-premises, PowerShell and Windows server. This contract involves ensuring the stability, performance, and security of SharePoint environments across the organization. The Contractor will work closely with the Modern Workplace Team Lead, IT teams, developers, and business stakeholders to support the effective use of SharePoint as a collaborative and document management platform. **Job Responsibilities/ Accountabilities of the Contract** : * Maintain and Monitor SharePoint Subscription Edition Farms: Oversee the daily operations of SharePoint farms, including monitoring system performance, applying updates, and troubleshooting issues. * Infrastructure Management: Manage the underlying infrastructure supporting SharePoint, including servers, databases, and network configurations. * Configuration and Optimization: Configure SharePoint services and settings to meet organizational needs, optimizing performance and scalability. * Security and Compliance: Implement and enforce security policies to protect sensitive information, including managing permissions, access controls, and compliance with data protection regulations. * PowerShell Automation: Use PowerShell scripts to automate routine tasks, streamline processes, and improve system efficiency. * Collaboration with Development Teams: Work with developers on deploying and managing custom solutions, web parts, and applications within the SharePoint environment. * Work directly with clients to support various projects and solutions. * dentify areas and processes where SharePoint and DevOps can be better leveraged and facilitate process improvement. * Working with DevOps Pipelines * To design end\-to\-end solutions that improve collaboration, productivity, and knowledge sharing. **Technical Requirements of the Successful Candidate** : * 5\+ years of experience configuring and maintaining SharePoint on\-premises farms and services. * Extensive experience with PowerShell scripts and automations. * Proven experience in building new farms and configuring SharePoint services. * Experience maintaining Active Directory (AD) * Experience with DevOps CI/CD pipelines. * Ability to get creative and innovative with solutions, offering alternative approaches to various challenges that may arise * Strong communication skills, with the ability to collaborate with cross\-functional teams * Attention to detail and a commitment to maintaining high standards of performance and security. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a dynamic environment. **Advantageous requirement:** * Software development experience with .NET, SPFx, React, and/or Angular is advantageous. * Experience with Azure services such as Entra Sync and multi\-tenant organization are advantageous.
